arguments.name: bubbletea-v2-migration
arguments.description: Migrate wasa-cli TUI to Bubble Tea v2
arguments.prompt:
You are working in the Go repository at /home/jcarlsson/.wasa/worktrees/0c1bb6a9d11f/task-141-upgrade-the-tui-to-new-bubble-tea-v2 (module github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli, Go 1.26.3).

## Task

Migrate the entire TUI layer from Bubble Tea v1 to v2. This is a pure dependency/API migration — NO user-visible behavior should change. The cockpit must look and behave exactly as it does today once done.

### Dependency swap (go.mod)

- `github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea v1.3.10` → `charm.land/bubbletea/v2` (latest v2.0.8, or newer)
- `github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles v1.0.0` → `charm.land/bubbles/v2` (latest v2.1.1, or newer)
- `github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss v1.1.0` → `charm.land/lipgloss/v2` (latest v2.0.5, or newer)
- Keep `github.com/charmbracelet/x/ansi` aligned with whatever `go mod tidy` resolves as transitive deps of the above (it may become indirect, or be replaced — let `go mod tidy` decide).

Run `go get charm.land/bubbletea/v2@latest charm.land/bubbles/v2@latest charm.land/lipgloss/v2@latest` then `go mod tidy`.

### Scope

All Charm imports live under `internal/tui` (root package, plus `internal/tui/theme`, `internal/tui/component`, `internal/tui/modal`, `internal/tui/pane`). There are no Charm imports outside `internal/tui`. Files currently importing v1 Charm modules (confirm this list is current via `grep -rl "charmbracelet/bubbletea\|charmbracelet/bubbles\|charmbracelet/lipgloss" internal cmd`):

- internal/tui/app.go, app_test.go, checkpoint_search.go, checkpoint_search_test.go, checkpoints.go, filter.go, filter_test.go, sessionlist.go, sessionlist_test.go, icons.go, notify.go, notify_test.go
- internal/tui/component/branch_picker.go, branch_picker_test.go, directory_picker.go, directory_picker_test.go, tabs.go, keymap.go, keymap_test.go, fuzzy.go, text.go, modal.go
- internal/tui/modal/config.go, config_test.go, confirm.go, create.go, create_test.go
- internal/tui/pane/diff.go, preview.go, tabbed.go, terminal.go, pane.go, pane_test.go
- internal/tui/theme/theme.go, theme_test.go

## Official migration guides — READ CAREFULLY, follow exactly (do not guess at API)

### Bubble Tea v1→v2 (charm.land/bubbletea/v2)

```
# Bubble Tea v2 Upgrade Guide

## Import Paths
// Before: import tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
// After:  import tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
// Before: import "github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
// After:  import "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"

## The Big Idea: Declarative Views
v1 used program options (tea.WithAltScreen()) and imperative commands (tea.EnterAltScreen) to toggle
terminal features. v2 uses declarative fields on the tea.View struct returned from View().

```go
// v1
p := tea.NewProgram(model{}, tea.WithAltScreen(), tea.WithMouseCellMotion())
// v2
func (m model) View() tea.View {
    v := tea.NewView("Hello!")
    v.AltScreen = true
    v.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
    return v
}
```

## View Returns a tea.View Now
View() no longer returns a string. It returns a tea.View struct.
```go
// Before: func (m model) View() string { return "Hello, world!" }
// After:  func (m model) View() tea.View { return tea.NewView("Hello, world!") }
```
Longer form:
```go
func (m model) View() tea.View {
    var v tea.View
    v.SetContent("Hello, world!")
    v.AltScreen = true
    return v
}
```
tea.View fields: Content, AltScreen, MouseMode (MouseModeNone/MouseModeCellMotion/MouseModeAllMotion),
ReportFocus, DisableBracketedPasteMode, WindowTitle, Cursor, ForegroundColor, BackgroundColor,
ProgressBar, KeyboardEnhancements, OnMouse.

## Key Messages
tea.KeyMsg is now an INTERFACE covering both presses and releases. For most code use tea.KeyPressMsg:
```go
// Before: case tea.KeyMsg: switch msg.String() { case "q": return m, tea.Quit }
// After:  case tea.KeyPressMsg: switch msg.String() { case "q": return m, tea.Quit }
```
To handle both: `case tea.KeyMsg: switch key := msg.(type) { case tea.KeyPressMsg: ...; case tea.KeyReleaseMsg: ... }`

Key field renames:
| v1          | v2                                    | Notes |
|-------------|----------------------------------------|-------|
| msg.Type    | msg.Code                                | rune, e.g. tea.KeyEnter, 'a' |
| msg.Runes   | msg.Text                                | now a string, not []rune |
| msg.Alt     | msg.Mod.Contains(tea.ModAlt)            | |
| tea.KeyRune | (removed)                                | check len(msg.Text) > 0 instead |
| tea.KeyCtrlC| (removed)                                | use msg.String() == "ctrl+c" or msg.Code=='c' && msg.Mod==tea.ModCtrl |

Space bar: msg.String() now returns "space" instead of " ". `case " ":` → `case "space":`. (msg.Code stays ' ', msg.Text stays " ".)

New key fields (no v1 equivalent): key.ShiftedCode, key.BaseCode, key.IsRepeat, key.Keystroke().

## Paste Messages
```go
// Before: case tea.KeyMsg: if msg.Paste { m.text += string(msg.Runes) }
// After:  case tea.PasteMsg: m.text += msg.Content
//         case tea.PasteStartMsg: // paste started
//         case tea.PasteEndMsg:   // paste ended
```

## Mouse Messages
tea.MouseMsg is now an INTERFACE. Get coords via msg.Mouse():
```go
// Before: case tea.MouseMsg: x, y := msg.X, msg.Y
// After:  case tea.MouseMsg: mouse := msg.Mouse(); x, y := mouse.X, mouse.Y
```
Split by type instead of checking msg.Action:
```go
// Before: case tea.MouseMsg: if msg.Action == tea.MouseActionPress && msg.Button == tea.MouseButtonLeft { ... }
// After:  case tea.MouseClickMsg: if msg.Button == tea.MouseLeft { ... }
//         case tea.MouseReleaseMsg: ...
//         case tea.MouseWheelMsg: ...
//         case tea.MouseMotionMsg: ...
```
Button renames: MouseButtonLeft→MouseLeft, MouseButtonRight→MouseRight, MouseButtonMiddle→MouseMiddle,
MouseButtonWheelUp→MouseWheelUp, MouseButtonWheelDown→MouseWheelDown, MouseButtonWheelLeft→MouseWheelLeft,
MouseButtonWheelRight→MouseWheelRight. tea.MouseEvent struct → tea.Mouse struct (fields X, Y, Button, Mod).
Mouse mode is now a View field (see above), not tea.WithMouseCellMotion()/tea.WithMouseAllMotion().

## Removed Program Options → View fields
tea.WithAltScreen() → view.AltScreen = true
tea.WithMouseCellMotion() → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
tea.WithMouseAllMotion() → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeAllMotion
tea.WithReportFocus() → view.ReportFocus = true
tea.WithoutBracketedPaste() → view.DisableBracketedPasteMode = true
tea.WithInputTTY() → just remove, v2 always opens TTY automatically
tea.WithANSICompressor() → just remove, renderer optimizes automatically

## Removed Commands → View fields
tea.EnterAltScreen/tea.ExitAltScreen → view.AltScreen = true/false
tea.EnableMouseCellMotion → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
tea.EnableMouseAllMotion → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeAllMotion
tea.DisableMouse → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeNone
tea.HideCursor → view.Cursor = nil
tea.ShowCursor → view.Cursor = &tea.Cursor{...} or tea.NewCursor(x, y)
tea.EnableBracketedPaste/tea.DisableBracketedPaste → view.DisableBracketedPasteMode = false/true
tea.EnableReportFocus/tea.DisableReportFocus → view.ReportFocus = true/false
tea.SetWindowTitle("...") → view.WindowTitle = "..."

## Removed Program Methods
p.Start()/p.StartReturningModel() → p.Run()
p.EnterAltScreen()/p.ExitAltScreen() → view.AltScreen field in View()
p.EnableMouseCellMotion()/p.DisableMouseCellMotion()/p.EnableMouseAllMotion()/p.DisableMouseAllMotion() → view.MouseMode field in View()
p.SetWindowTitle(...) → view.WindowTitle field in View()

## Renamed APIs
tea.Sequentially(...) → tea.Sequence(...)
tea.WindowSize() → tea.RequestWindowSize (now returns Msg directly, not Cmd)

## New Program Options
tea.WithColorProfile(p), tea.WithWindowSize(w, h)
```

### Lip Gloss v1→v2 (charm.land/lipgloss/v2)

```
## Module Path
github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss → charm.land/lipgloss/v2
github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/table → charm.land/lipgloss/v2/table
github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/tree → charm.land/lipgloss/v2/tree
github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/list → charm.land/lipgloss/v2/list

## Color System — the biggest change
Color is now a FUNCTION returning image/color.Color, not a string type:
```go
// v1: var c lipgloss.Color = "21"
// v2: var c color.Color = lipgloss.Color("21")   // Color() is a func call
```
All Style methods that took `lipgloss.TerminalColor` now take `image/color.Color` (add `import "image/color"`):
Foreground(c color.Color), Background(c color.Color), BorderForeground(c ...color.Color), etc.
`lipgloss.TerminalColor` interface is REMOVED — replace every occurrence with `color.Color`.

ANSIColor is now `type ANSIColor = ansi.IndexedColor` (alias). v2 exports named constants:
lipgloss.Black, Red, Green, Yellow, Blue, Magenta, Cyan, White, BrightBlack..BrightWhite.

AdaptiveColor / CompleteColor / CompleteAdaptiveColor moved out of root package. Use the `compat`
package (charm.land/lipgloss/v2/compat) for a drop-in replacement — THIS IS THE RECOMMENDED QUICK PATH
for this migration since we must preserve exact current visual behavior with minimal risk:
```go
// v1: color := lipgloss.AdaptiveColor{Light: "#0000ff", Dark: "#000099"}
// v2 quick path:
import "charm.land/lipgloss/v2/compat"
color := compat.AdaptiveColor{
    Light: lipgloss.Color("#0000ff"),
    Dark:  lipgloss.Color("#000099"),
}
```
Same drop-in pattern for CompleteColor → compat.CompleteColor{TrueColor, ANSI256, ANSI: all lipgloss.Color(...) now}.
NOTE: compat.AdaptiveColor/CompleteColor take color.Color-typed fields (i.e. lipgloss.Color("...") calls), not bare strings.

## Renderer Removal
lipgloss.Renderer type and ALL renderer functions/methods are REMOVED: DefaultRenderer(), SetDefaultRenderer(r),
NewRenderer(w, opts...), ColorProfile(), SetColorProfile(p), renderer.NewStyle().
Style is now a plain value type with no renderer. Replace `lipgloss.DefaultRenderer().NewStyle()` or
`renderer.NewStyle()` with plain `lipgloss.NewStyle()`. Remove any *Renderer fields from our types.

## Printing / Color Downsampling
Render() always emits full-fidelity ANSI now; downsampling happens at print. If we call fmt.Println(style.Render(...))
directly to a terminal outside of Bubble Tea, switch to lipgloss.Println/lipgloss.Fprintln/lipgloss.Sprint.
**With Bubble Tea v2, no changes needed — Bubble Tea handles downsampling internally.** (Our code renders inside
a Bubble Tea View(), so this should mostly be a non-issue — verify no direct fmt.Print of styled output outside
the TUI event loop.)

## Background Detection
lipgloss.HasDarkBackground() (no args) → lipgloss.HasDarkBackground(os.Stdin, os.Stdout) (now takes io args).
New helper: lightDark := lipgloss.LightDark(hasDark); color := lightDark(lightColor, darkColor).
Within Bubble Tea: request via tea.RequestBackgroundColor in Init(), handle tea.BackgroundColorMsg in Update()
with msg.IsDark(). Prefer the `compat` package (compat.HasDarkBackground()) ONLY if there's currently a
call-site outside the Bubble Tea loop; inside the loop prefer the tea.RequestBackgroundColor pattern if adding
new logic, but if current code has no background-adaptive theming at all, do not add any — preserve current behavior.

## Whitespace Options
lipgloss.WithWhitespaceForeground(c) + WithWhitespaceBackground(c) → single
lipgloss.WithWhitespaceStyle(lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(c).Background(c))

## Underline
Underline(true) still works. New: UnderlineStyle(lipgloss.UnderlineCurly), UnderlineColor(color.Color).

## Style API Changes
NewStyle() no longer tied to a renderer — always `lipgloss.NewStyle()`.
GetForeground()/GetBackground() etc. now return color.Color instead of TerminalColor.
New methods (not required unless matching existing behavior needs them): UnderlineStyle, UnderlineColor,
PaddingChar, MarginChar, Hyperlink, BorderForegroundBlend, BorderForegroundBlendOffset.

## Removed APIs quick table
Renderer type, DefaultRenderer(), SetDefaultRenderer(r), NewRenderer(w,opts...) → all removed, not needed.
ColorProfile() → colorprofile.Detect(w, env) (only if truly needed; usually not needed inside Bubble Tea).
SetColorProfile(p) → not needed.
HasDarkBackground() (no args) → lipgloss.HasDarkBackground(in, out).
SetHasDarkBackground(b) → not needed.
type TerminalColor → image/color.Color.
type Color string → func Color(string) color.Color.
type ANSIColor uint → type ANSIColor = ansi.IndexedColor.
type AdaptiveColor → compat.AdaptiveColor or LightDark.
type CompleteColor → compat.CompleteColor or Complete.
type CompleteAdaptiveColor → compat.CompleteAdaptiveColor.
WithWhitespaceForeground(c)/WithWhitespaceBackground(c) → WithWhitespaceStyle(s).
renderer.NewStyle() → lipgloss.NewStyle().
```

### Bubbles v1→v2 (charm.land/bubbles/v2)

```
## Import Paths
github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/X → charm.land/bubbles/v2/X for every subpackage (cursor, help, key, list,
paginator, progress, spinner, stopwatch, table, textarea, textinput, timer, viewport).
NOTE: runeutil and memoization packages are now internal and NOT importable — if our code imports them,
find an alternative (unlikely used, but check).

## Global Patterns
- tea.KeyMsg → tea.KeyPressMsg everywhere bubbles-adjacent code switches on key messages.
- Many components replaced exported Width/Height fields with getter/setter methods:
  m.Width = 40 → m.SetWidth(40); fmt.Println(m.Width) → fmt.Println(m.Width()).
  Affected: filepicker, help, progress, table, textinput, viewport.
- DefaultKeyMap variables are now functions: textinput.DefaultKeyMap → textinput.DefaultKeyMap().
  Affected: paginator, textarea, textinput.
- AdaptiveColor removed → style funcs that auto-adapted now take explicit isDark bool (see Section 4 below).
- All NewModel aliases removed — use New() directly (help, list, paginator, spinner, textinput).

## Per-component notes relevant here (check which of these components our code actually uses first!)
- key package: same import path change, API otherwise stable for key.Binding/key.NewBinding/key.Matches —
  BUT key.Matches now takes tea.KeyPressMsg instead of tea.KeyMsg since KeyMsg is an interface. Check charm.land/bubbles/v2/key godoc/source if in doubt.
- textinput: DefaultKeyMap is now a func; Width is now SetWidth()/Width(); style fields moved into a
  Styles struct (Model.PromptStyle → StyleState.Prompt via ti.Styles().Focused.Prompt, etc. — use
  ti.SetStyles(styles) / ti.Styles() and textinput.DefaultStyles(isDark)); Model.Cursor (cursor.Model
  field) → Model.Cursor() (func returning *tea.Cursor).
- viewport: New(w, h int) → New(viewport.WithWidth(w), viewport.WithHeight(h)) or New() + SetWidth/SetHeight;
  Width/Height/YOffset fields → SetWidth()/Width(), SetHeight()/Height(), SetYOffset()/YOffset();
  HighPerformanceRendering removed entirely.
- list, help, spinner, table, progress, paginator: check per-component table in the full guide text above
  if our code imports them (grep for exact bubbles subpackage imports first).

## Light and Dark Styles
Since AdaptiveColor is gone, any bubbles component styling that used to auto-adapt to terminal background
now needs an explicit isDark bool, OR — since our theme.go likely already defines static colors rather than
relying on this auto-detection — check `internal/tui/theme/theme.go` first: if it defines its own fixed
palette (not relying on lipgloss.AdaptiveColor / bubbles auto dark-detection), then this section mostly
doesn't apply to us. Only add tea.RequestBackgroundColor / isDark plumbing if current code already does
background-adaptive theming — do NOT introduce new behavior.
```

## Critical constraints (from the AGENTS.md conventions of this repo)

- `internal/tui/theme` is a LEAF package (only imports config + lipgloss). Concentrate the lipgloss v2
  color-API migration there so the rest of the TUI keeps importing `theme.Theme` unchanged — i.e. other
  packages should NOT need `image/color` imports if `theme.Theme` continues to expose lipgloss.Color-typed
  or pre-resolved style fields the same way it does today. Inspect theme.go's current public API/shape
  before changing it, and preserve it as much as possible; only touch what the v1→v2 diff forces you to touch.
- `component`/`modal`/`pane` packages must NEVER import the root `tui` package (no import cycles). Do not
  introduce a cycle to work around a v2 API change.
- Pickers (branch_picker.go, directory_picker.go) and colour/key editors in modal/config.go are bespoke —
  do NOT refactor them onto `bubbles/list`. Port them as-is onto the v2 API, preserving their exact current
  UX/behavior.
- No narrative inline comments; only non-obvious constraints. Exported types/funcs need doc comments
  (revive lint enforces this) — don't remove existing doc comments.
- Package docs live in doc.go files only — don't move them.
- This is a **pure migration**: don't change behavior, don't refactor unrelated code, don't fix unrelated
  pre-existing issues.

## Workflow — iterate methodically

1. First, read AGENTS.md at the repo root and internal/tui/theme/theme.go in full to understand the current
   theme API shape (Theme struct, exported style fields, color constants) since that's the leaf you must
   preserve the external shape of.
2. Update go.mod/go.sum: `go get charm.land/bubbletea/v2@latest charm.land/bubbles/v2@latest charm.land/lipgloss/v2@latest && go mod tidy`.
3. Migrate `internal/tui/theme/theme.go` first (the leaf) — swap lipgloss import, fix Color()/TerminalColor→color.Color usages, AdaptiveColor→compat.AdaptiveColor if used, keep the same exported Theme struct/fields/methods signature wherever possible so downstream files compile unchanged against theme.Theme usage. Then fix theme_test.go.
4. Migrate `internal/tui/component/*.go` next (keymap.go and its _test.go first since keymap dispatch
   patterns — tea.KeyMsg switches — repeat across many files and this file likely defines the shared
   keymap abstraction used everywhere), then tabs.go, fuzzy.go, text.go, modal.go, branch_picker.go,
   directory_picker.go and their _test.go files.
5. Migrate `internal/tui/modal/*.go` (confirm.go, create.go, config.go) and their _test.go files.
6. Migrate `internal/tui/pane/*.go` (pane.go, preview.go, diff.go, terminal.go, tabbed.go) and pane_test.go.
7. Migrate the root `internal/tui/*.go` files: app.go (Program construction, Init/Update/View, key
   dispatch — this is the biggest and most central file), filter.go, sessionlist.go, checkpoints.go,
   checkpoint_search.go, notify.go, icons.go, and all their _test.go files.
8. After each package (or logical group) compiles, run `go build ./...` to catch cross-package breakage
   early rather than waiting until the very end.
9. Once everything compiles: run `gofmt -l .` and fix formatting; run `goimports -w .` if available
   (check with `which goimports`, it may need `make install` first — if unavailable just ensure imports
   are well-formed manually).
10. Run `go vet ./...` and fix any issues.
11. Run `golangci-lint run ./...` if available (check with `which golangci-lint`; if missing, note it
    and skip — do not attempt to install new linting tools).
12. Run `go test ./...` and fix all failures. Test files use the same tea/bubbles/lipgloss v1 APIs and
    need the same treatment — update test helpers, mock messages (tea.KeyMsg{...} literals → tea.KeyPressMsg{...}),
    and any assertions on renamed fields (msg.Type→msg.Code, msg.Runes→msg.Text, etc).
13. Run `go build -buildvcs=false -o bin/wasa ./cmd/wasa` (mirrors the Makefile's `make build` target) to
    confirm the final binary builds.
14. Finally, run `grep -rn "charmbracelet/bubbletea\|charmbracelet/bubbles\|charmbracelet/lipgloss" internal cmd`
    and confirm there are ZERO matches (aside from any go.sum comment lines, which don't matter — only
    check .go files). Also confirm go.mod no longer lists any charmbracelet bubbletea/bubbles/lipgloss
    v1 requirement (indirect deps like charmbracelet/colorprofile, charmbracelet/x/ansi, charmbracelet/x/term,
    charmbracelet/x/cellbuf are fine to keep as transitive deps if go mod tidy keeps them).

## Verification commands (must all pass before you finish)

```sh
cd /home/jcarlsson/.wasa/worktrees/0c1bb6a9d11f/task-141-upgrade-the-tui-to-new-bubble-tea-v2
go build ./...
go vet ./...
go test ./...
go build -buildvcs=false -o bin/wasa ./cmd/wasa
grep -rn "charmbracelet/bubbletea\|charmbracelet/bubbles\|charmbracelet/lipgloss" internal cmd --include=*.go
```
The grep must return no output. Do not consider the task done until build, vet, and test all pass and
that grep is empty. If `make lint` (golangci-lint) is available, run it too and fix any new issues it
flags that are directly caused by your changes (pre-existing lint issues unrelated to this migration are
out of scope).

## Final report

When done, report: (a) confirmation of go.mod's new require lines for the three modules, (b) the exact
versions resolved, (c) pass/fail status of build/vet/test/lint, (d) any behavior-preservation judgment
calls you had to make (e.g. how you handled AdaptiveColor if theme.go used it, or any place where the v1
and v2 APIs don't map 1:1 and you had to choose an approach) so they can be reviewed, and (e) a short list
of every file you touched.

## Task

Migrate the entire TUI layer from Bubble Tea v1 to v2. This is a pure dependency/API migration — NO user-visible behavior should change. The cockpit must look and behave exactly as it does today once done.

### Dependency swap (go.mod)

- `github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea v1.3.10` → `charm.land/bubbletea/v2` (latest v2.0.8, or newer)
- `github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles v1.0.0` → `charm.land/bubbles/v2` (latest v2.1.1, or newer)
- `github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss v1.1.0` → `charm.land/lipgloss/v2` (latest v2.0.5, or newer)
- Keep `github.com/charmbracelet/x/ansi` aligned with whatever `go mod tidy` resolves as transitive deps of the above (it may become indirect, or be replaced — let `go mod tidy` decide).

Run `go get charm.land/bubbletea/v2@latest charm.land/bubbles/v2@latest charm.land/lipgloss/v2@latest` then `go mod tidy`.

### Scope

All Charm imports live under `internal/tui` (root package, plus `internal/tui/theme`, `internal/tui/component`, `internal/tui/modal`, `internal/tui/pane`). There are no Charm imports outside `internal/tui`. Files currently importing v1 Charm modules (confirm this list is current via `grep -rl "charmbracelet/bubbletea\|charmbracelet/bubbles\|charmbracelet/lipgloss" internal cmd`):

- internal/tui/app.go, app_test.go, checkpoint_search.go, checkpoint_search_test.go, checkpoints.go, filter.go, filter_test.go, sessionlist.go, sessionlist_test.go, icons.go, notify.go, notify_test.go
- internal/tui/component/branch_picker.go, branch_picker_test.go, directory_picker.go, directory_picker_test.go, tabs.go, keymap.go, keymap_test.go, fuzzy.go, text.go, modal.go
- internal/tui/modal/config.go, config_test.go, confirm.go, create.go, create_test.go
- internal/tui/pane/diff.go, preview.go, tabbed.go, terminal.go, pane.go, pane_test.go
- internal/tui/theme/theme.go, theme_test.go

## Official migration guides — READ CAREFULLY, follow exactly (do not guess at API)

### Bubble Tea v1→v2 (charm.land/bubbletea/v2)

```
# Bubble Tea v2 Upgrade Guide

## Import Paths
// Before: import tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
// After:  import tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
// Before: import "github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
// After:  import "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"

## The Big Idea: Declarative Views
v1 used program options (tea.WithAltScreen()) and imperative commands (tea.EnterAltScreen) to toggle
terminal features. v2 uses declarative fields on the tea.View struct returned from View().

```go
// v1
p := tea.NewProgram(model{}, tea.WithAltScreen(), tea.WithMouseCellMotion())
// v2
func (m model) View() tea.View {
    v := tea.NewView("Hello!")
    v.AltScreen = true
    v.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
    return v
}
```

## View Returns a tea.View Now
View() no longer returns a string. It returns a tea.View struct.
```go
// Before: func (m model) View() string { return "Hello, world!" }
// After:  func (m model) View() tea.View { return tea.NewView("Hello, world!") }
```
Longer form:
```go
func (m model) View() tea.View {
    var v tea.View
    v.SetContent("Hello, world!")
    v.AltScreen = true
    return v
}
```
tea.View fields: Content, AltScreen, MouseMode (MouseModeNone/MouseModeCellMotion/MouseModeAllMotion),
ReportFocus, DisableBracketedPasteMode, WindowTitle, Cursor, ForegroundColor, BackgroundColor,
ProgressBar, KeyboardEnhancements, OnMouse.

## Key Messages
tea.KeyMsg is now an INTERFACE covering both presses and releases. For most code use tea.KeyPressMsg:
```go
// Before: case tea.KeyMsg: switch msg.String() { case "q": return m, tea.Quit }
// After:  case tea.KeyPressMsg: switch msg.String() { case "q": return m, tea.Quit }
```
To handle both: `case tea.KeyMsg: switch key := msg.(type) { case tea.KeyPressMsg: ...; case tea.KeyReleaseMsg: ... }`

Key field renames:
| v1          | v2                                    | Notes |
|-------------|----------------------------------------|-------|
| msg.Type    | msg.Code                                | rune, e.g. tea.KeyEnter, 'a' |
| msg.Runes   | msg.Text                                | now a string, not []rune |
| msg.Alt     | msg.Mod.Contains(tea.ModAlt)            | |
| tea.KeyRune | (removed)                                | check len(msg.Text) > 0 instead |
| tea.KeyCtrlC| (removed)                                | use msg.String() == "ctrl+c" or msg.Code=='c' && msg.Mod==tea.ModCtrl |

Space bar: msg.String() now returns "space" instead of " ". `case " ":` → `case "space":`. (msg.Code stays ' ', msg.Text stays " ".)

New key fields (no v1 equivalent): key.ShiftedCode, key.BaseCode, key.IsRepeat, key.Keystroke().

## Paste Messages
```go
// Before: case tea.KeyMsg: if msg.Paste { m.text += string(msg.Runes) }
// After:  case tea.PasteMsg: m.text += msg.Content
//         case tea.PasteStartMsg: // paste started
//         case tea.PasteEndMsg:   // paste ended
```

## Mouse Messages
tea.MouseMsg is now an INTERFACE. Get coords via msg.Mouse():
```go
// Before: case tea.MouseMsg: x, y := msg.X, msg.Y
// After:  case tea.MouseMsg: mouse := msg.Mouse(); x, y := mouse.X, mouse.Y
```
Split by type instead of checking msg.Action:
```go
// Before: case tea.MouseMsg: if msg.Action == tea.MouseActionPress && msg.Button == tea.MouseButtonLeft { ... }
// After:  case tea.MouseClickMsg: if msg.Button == tea.MouseLeft { ... }
//         case tea.MouseReleaseMsg: ...
//         case tea.MouseWheelMsg: ...
//         case tea.MouseMotionMsg: ...
```
Button renames: MouseButtonLeft→MouseLeft, MouseButtonRight→MouseRight, MouseButtonMiddle→MouseMiddle,
MouseButtonWheelUp→MouseWheelUp, MouseButtonWheelDown→MouseWheelDown, MouseButtonWheelLeft→MouseWheelLeft,
MouseButtonWheelRight→MouseWheelRight. tea.MouseEvent struct → tea.Mouse struct (fields X, Y, Button, Mod).
Mouse mode is now a View field (see above), not tea.WithMouseCellMotion()/tea.WithMouseAllMotion().

## Removed Program Options → View fields
tea.WithAltScreen() → view.AltScreen = true
tea.WithMouseCellMotion() → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
tea.WithMouseAllMotion() → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeAllMotion
tea.WithReportFocus() → view.ReportFocus = true
tea.WithoutBracketedPaste() → view.DisableBracketedPasteMode = true
tea.WithInputTTY() → just remove, v2 always opens TTY automatically
tea.WithANSICompressor() → just remove, renderer optimizes automatically

## Removed Commands → View fields
tea.EnterAltScreen/tea.ExitAltScreen → view.AltScreen = true/false
tea.EnableMouseCellMotion → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
tea.EnableMouseAllMotion → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeAllMotion
tea.DisableMouse → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeNone
tea.HideCursor → view.Cursor = nil
tea.ShowCursor → view.Cursor = &tea.Cursor{...} or tea.NewCursor(x, y)
tea.EnableBracketedPaste/tea.DisableBracketedPaste → view.DisableBracketedPasteMode = false/true
tea.EnableReportFocus/tea.DisableReportFocus → view.ReportFocus = true/false
tea.SetWindowTitle("...") → view.WindowTitle = "..."

## Removed Program Methods
p.Start()/p.StartReturningModel() → p.Run()
p.EnterAltScreen()/p.ExitAltScreen() → view.AltScreen field in View()
p.EnableMouseCellMotion()/p.DisableMouseCellMotion()/p.EnableMouseAllMotion()/p.DisableMouseAllMotion() → view.MouseMode field in View()
p.SetWindowTitle(...) → view.WindowTitle field in View()

## Renamed APIs
tea.Sequentially(...) → tea.Sequence(...)
tea.WindowSize() → tea.RequestWindowSize (now returns Msg directly, not Cmd)

## New Program Options
tea.WithColorProfile(p), tea.WithWindowSize(w, h)
```

## Whitespace Options

## Style API Changes
NewStyle() no longer tied to a renderer — always `lipgloss.NewStyle()`.
GetForeground()/GetBackground() etc. now return color.Color instead of TerminalColor.
New methods (not required unless matching existing behavior needs them): UnderlineStyle, UnderlineColor,
PaddingChar, MarginChar, Hyperlink, BorderForegroundBlend, BorderForegroundBlendOffset.

## Removed APIs quick table
Renderer type, DefaultRenderer(), SetDefaultRenderer(r), NewRenderer(w,opts...) → all removed, not needed.
ColorProfile() → colorprofile.Detect(w, env) (only if truly needed; usually not needed inside Bubble Tea).
SetColorProfile(p) → not needed.
HasDarkBackground() (no args) → lipgloss.HasDarkBackground(in, out).
SetHasDarkBackground(b) → not needed.
type TerminalColor → image/color.Color.
type Color string → func Color(string) color.Color.
type ANSIColor uint → type ANSIColor = ansi.IndexedColor.
type AdaptiveColor → compat.AdaptiveColor or LightDark.
type CompleteColor → compat.CompleteColor or Complete.
type CompleteAdaptiveColor → compat.CompleteAdaptiveColor.
WithWhitespaceForeground(c)/WithWhitespaceBackground(c) → WithWhitespaceStyle(s).
renderer.NewStyle() → lipgloss.NewStyle().
```

### Bubbles v1→v2 (charm.land/bubbles/v2)

```
## Import Paths
github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/X → charm.land/bubbles/v2/X for every subpackage (cursor, help, key, list,
paginator, progress, spinner, stopwatch, table, textarea, textinput, timer, viewport).
NOTE: runeutil and memoization packages are now internal and NOT importable — if our code imports them,
find an alternative (unlikely used, but check).

## Global Patterns
- tea.KeyMsg → tea.KeyPressMsg everywhere bubbles-adjacent code switches on key messages.
- Many components replaced exported Width/Height fields with getter/setter methods:
  m.Width = 40 → m.SetWidth(40); fmt.Println(m.Width) → fmt.Println(m.Width()).
  Affected: filepicker, help, progress, table, textinput, viewport.
- DefaultKeyMap variables are now functions: textinput.DefaultKeyMap → textinput.DefaultKeyMap().
  Affected: paginator, textarea, textinput.
- AdaptiveColor removed → style funcs that auto-adapted now take explicit isDark bool (see Section 4 below).
- All NewModel aliases removed — use New() directly (help, list, paginator, spinner, textinput).

## Per-component notes relevant here (check which of these components our code actually uses first!)
- key package: same import path change, API otherwise stable for key.Binding/key.NewBinding/key.Matches —
  BUT key.Matches now takes tea.KeyPressMsg instead of tea.KeyMsg since KeyMsg is an interface. Check charm.land/bubbles/v2/key godoc/source if in doubt.
- textinput: DefaultKeyMap is now a func; Width is now SetWidth()/Width(); style fields moved into a
  Styles struct (Model.PromptStyle → StyleState.Prompt via ti.Styles().Focused.Prompt, etc. — use
  ti.SetStyles(styles) / ti.Styles() and textinput.DefaultStyles(isDark)); Model.Cursor (cursor.Model
  field) → Model.Cursor() (func returning *tea.Cursor).
- viewport: New(w, h int) → New(viewport.WithWidth(w), viewport.WithHeight(h)) or New() + SetWidth/SetHeight;
  Width/Height/YOffset fields → SetWidth()/Width(), SetHeight()/Height(), SetYOffset()/YOffset();
  HighPerformanceRendering removed entirely.
- list, help, spinner, table, progress, paginator: check per-component table in the full guide text above
  if our code imports them (grep for exact bubbles subpackage imports first).

## Light and Dark Styles
Since AdaptiveColor is gone, any bubbles component styling that used to auto-adapt to terminal background
now needs an explicit isDark bool, OR — since our theme.go likely already defines static colors rather than
relying on this auto-detection — check `internal/tui/theme/theme.go` first: if it defines its own fixed
palette (not relying on lipgloss.AdaptiveColor / bubbles auto dark-detection), then this section mostly
doesn't apply to us. Only add tea.RequestBackgroundColor / isDark plumbing if current code already does
background-adaptive theming — do NOT introduce new behavior.
```

## Official migration guides — READ CAREFULLY, follow exactly (do not guess at API)

### Bubble Tea v1→v2 (charm.land/bubbletea/v2)

```
# Bubble Tea v2 Upgrade Guide

## Import Paths
// Before: import tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
// After:  import tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
// Before: import "github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
// After:  import "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"

## The Big Idea: Declarative Views
v1 used program options (tea.WithAltScreen()) and imperative commands (tea.EnterAltScreen) to toggle
terminal features. v2 uses declarative fields on the tea.View struct returned from View().

```go
// v1
p := tea.NewProgram(model{}, tea.WithAltScreen(), tea.WithMouseCellMotion())
// v2
func (m model) View() tea.View {
    v := tea.NewView("Hello!")
    v.AltScreen = true
    v.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
    return v
}
```

## View Returns a tea.View Now
View() no longer returns a string. It returns a tea.View struct.
```go
// Before: func (m model) View() string { return "Hello, world!" }
// After:  func (m model) View() tea.View { return tea.NewView("Hello, world!") }
```
Longer form:
```go
func (m model) View() tea.View {
    var v tea.View
    v.SetContent("Hello, world!")
    v.AltScreen = true
    return v
}
```
tea.View fields: Content, AltScreen, MouseMode (MouseModeNone/MouseModeCellMotion/MouseModeAllMotion),
ReportFocus, DisableBracketedPasteMode, WindowTitle, Cursor, ForegroundColor, BackgroundColor,
ProgressBar, KeyboardEnhancements, OnMouse.

## Key Messages
tea.KeyMsg is now an INTERFACE covering both presses and releases. For most code use tea.KeyPressMsg:
```go
// Before: case tea.KeyMsg: switch msg.String() { case "q": return m, tea.Quit }
// After:  case tea.KeyPressMsg: switch msg.String() { case "q": return m, tea.Quit }
```
To handle both: `case tea.KeyMsg: switch key := msg.(type) { case tea.KeyPressMsg: ...; case tea.KeyReleaseMsg: ... }`

Key field renames:
| v1          | v2                                    | Notes |
|-------------|----------------------------------------|-------|
| msg.Type    | msg.Code                                | rune, e.g. tea.KeyEnter, 'a' |
| msg.Runes   | msg.Text                                | now a string, not []rune |
| msg.Alt     | msg.Mod.Contains(tea.ModAlt)            | |
| tea.KeyRune | (removed)                                | check len(msg.Text) > 0 instead |
| tea.KeyCtrlC| (removed)                                | use msg.String() == "ctrl+c" or msg.Code=='c' && msg.Mod==tea.ModCtrl |

Space bar: msg.String() now returns "space" instead of " ". `case " ":` → `case "space":`. (msg.Code stays ' ', msg.Text stays " ".)

New key fields (no v1 equivalent): key.ShiftedCode, key.BaseCode, key.IsRepeat, key.Keystroke().

## Paste Messages
```go
// Before: case tea.KeyMsg: if msg.Paste { m.text += string(msg.Runes) }
// After:  case tea.PasteMsg: m.text += msg.Content
//         case tea.PasteStartMsg: // paste started
//         case tea.PasteEndMsg:   // paste ended
```

## Mouse Messages
tea.MouseMsg is now an INTERFACE. Get coords via msg.Mouse():
```go
// Before: case tea.MouseMsg: x, y := msg.X, msg.Y
// After:  case tea.MouseMsg: mouse := msg.Mouse(); x, y := mouse.X, mouse.Y
```
Split by type instead of checking msg.Action:
```go
// Before: case tea.MouseMsg: if msg.Action == tea.MouseActionPress && msg.Button == tea.MouseButtonLeft { ... }
// After:  case tea.MouseClickMsg: if msg.Button == tea.MouseLeft { ... }
//         case tea.MouseReleaseMsg: ...
//         case tea.MouseWheelMsg: ...
//         case tea.MouseMotionMsg: ...
```
Button renames: MouseButtonLeft→MouseLeft, MouseButtonRight→MouseRight, MouseButtonMiddle→MouseMiddle,
MouseButtonWheelUp→MouseWheelUp, MouseButtonWheelDown→MouseWheelDown, MouseButtonWheelLeft→MouseWheelLeft,
MouseButtonWheelRight→MouseWheelRight. tea.MouseEvent struct → tea.Mouse struct (fields X, Y, Button, Mod).
Mouse mode is now a View field (see above), not tea.WithMouseCellMotion()/tea.WithMouseAllMotion().

## Removed Program Options → View fields
tea.WithAltScreen() → view.AltScreen = true
tea.WithMouseCellMotion() → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
tea.WithMouseAllMotion() → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeAllMotion
tea.WithReportFocus() → view.ReportFocus = true
tea.WithoutBracketedPaste() → view.DisableBracketedPasteMode = true
tea.WithInputTTY() → just remove, v2 always opens TTY automatically
tea.WithANSICompressor() → just remove, renderer optimizes automatically

## Removed Commands → View fields
tea.EnterAltScreen/tea.ExitAltScreen → view.AltScreen = true/false
tea.EnableMouseCellMotion → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeCellMotion
tea.EnableMouseAllMotion → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeAllMotion
tea.DisableMouse → view.MouseMode = tea.MouseModeNone
tea.HideCursor → view.Cursor = nil
tea.ShowCursor → view.Cursor = &tea.Cursor{...} or tea.NewCursor(x, y)
tea.EnableBracketedPaste/tea.DisableBracketedPaste → view.DisableBracketedPasteMode = false/true
tea.EnableReportFocus/tea.DisableReportFocus → view.ReportFocus = true/false
tea.SetWindowTitle("...") → view.WindowTitle = "..."

## Removed Program Methods
p.Start()/p.StartReturningModel() → p.Run()
p.EnterAltScreen()/p.ExitAltScreen() → view.AltScreen field in View()
p.EnableMouseCellMotion()/p.DisableMouseCellMotion()/p.EnableMouseAllMotion()/p.DisableMouseAllMotion() → view.MouseMode field in View()
p.SetWindowTitle(...) → view.WindowTitle field in View()

## Renamed APIs
tea.Sequentially(...) → tea.Sequence(...)
tea.WindowSize() → tea.RequestWindowSize (now returns Msg directly, not Cmd)

## New Program Options
tea.WithColorProfile(p), tea.WithWindowSize(w, h)
```

### Lip Gloss v1→v2 (charm.land/lipgloss/v2)

```
## Module Path
github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss → charm.land/lipgloss/v2
github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/table → charm.land/lipgloss/v2/table
github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/tree → charm.land/lipgloss/v2/tree
github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/list → charm.land/lipgloss/v2/list

## Color System — the biggest change
Color is now a FUNCTION returning image/color.Color, not a string type:
```go
// v1: var c lipgloss.Color = "21"
// v2: var c color.Color = lipgloss.Color("21")   // Color() is a func call
```
All Style methods that took `lipgloss.TerminalColor` now take `image/color.Color` (add `import "image/color"`):
Foreground(c color.Color), Background(c color.Color), BorderForeground(c ...color.Color), etc.
`lipgloss.TerminalColor` interface is REMOVED — replace every occurrence with `color.Color`.

ANSIColor is now `type ANSIColor = ansi.IndexedColor` (alias). v2 exports named constants:
lipgloss.Black, Red, Green, Yellow, Blue, Magenta, Cyan, White, BrightBlack..BrightWhite.

AdaptiveColor / CompleteColor / CompleteAdaptiveColor moved out of root package. Use the `compat`
package (charm.land/lipgloss/v2/compat) for a drop-in replacement — THIS IS THE RECOMMENDED QUICK PATH
for this migration since we must preserve exact current visual behavior with minimal risk:
```go
// v1: color := lipgloss.AdaptiveColor{Light: "#0000ff", Dark: "#000099"}
// v2 quick path:
import "charm.land/lipgloss/v2/compat"
color := compat.AdaptiveColor{
    Light: lipgloss.Color("#0000ff"),
    Dark:  lipgloss.Color("#000099"),
}
```
Same drop-in pattern for CompleteColor → compat.CompleteColor{TrueColor, ANSI256, ANSI: all lipgloss.Color(...) now}.
NOTE: compat.AdaptiveColor/CompleteColor take color.Color-typed fields (i.e. lipgloss.Color("...") calls), not bare strings.

## Renderer Removal
lipgloss.Renderer type and ALL renderer functions/methods are REMOVED: DefaultRenderer(), SetDefaultRenderer(r),
NewRenderer(w, opts...), ColorProfile(), SetColorProfile(p), renderer.NewStyle().
Style is now a plain value type with no renderer. Replace `lipgloss.DefaultRenderer().NewStyle()` or
`renderer.NewStyle()` with plain `lipgloss.NewStyle()`. Remove any *Renderer fields from our types.

## Printing / Color Downsampling
Render() always emits full-fidelity ANSI now; downsampling happens at print. If we call fmt.Println(style.Render(...))
directly to a terminal outside of Bubble Tea, switch to lipgloss.Println/lipgloss.Fprintln/lipgloss.Sprint.
**With Bubble Tea v2, no changes needed — Bubble Tea handles downsampling internally.** (Our code renders inside
a Bubble Tea View(), so this should mostly be a non-issue — verify no direct fmt.Print of styled output outside
the TUI event loop.)

## Background Detection
lipgloss.HasDarkBackground() (no args) → lipgloss.HasDarkBackground(os.Stdin, os.Stdout) (now takes io args).
New helper: lightDark := lipgloss.LightDark(hasDark); color := lightDark(lightColor, darkColor).
Within Bubble Tea: request via tea.RequestBackgroundColor in Init(), handle tea.BackgroundColorMsg in Update()
with msg.IsDark(). Prefer the `compat` package (compat.HasDarkBackground()) ONLY if there's currently a
call-site outside the Bubble Tea loop; inside the loop prefer the tea.RequestBackgroundColor pattern if adding
new logic, but if current code has no background-adaptive theming at all, do not add any — preserve current behavior.

## Whitespace Options
lipgloss.WithWhitespaceForeground(c) + WithWhitespaceBackground(c) → single
lipgloss.WithWhitespaceStyle(lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(c).Background(c))

## Underline
Underline(true) still works. New: UnderlineStyle(lipgloss.UnderlineCurly), UnderlineColor(color.Color).

## Style API Changes
NewStyle() no longer tied to a renderer — always `lipgloss.NewStyle()`.
GetForeground()/GetBackground() etc. now return color.Color instead of TerminalColor.
New methods (not required unless matching existing behavior needs them): UnderlineStyle, UnderlineColor,
PaddingChar, MarginChar, Hyperlink, BorderForegroundBlend, BorderForegroundBlendOffset.

## Removed APIs quick table
Renderer type, DefaultRenderer(), SetDefaultRenderer(r), NewRenderer(w,opts...) → all removed, not needed.
ColorProfile() → colorprofile.Detect(w, env) (only if truly needed; usually not needed inside Bubble Tea).
SetColorProfile(p) → not needed.
HasDarkBackground() (no args) → lipgloss.HasDarkBackground(in, out).
SetHasDarkBackground(b) → not needed.
type TerminalColor → image/color.Color.
type Color string → func Color(string) color.Color.
type ANSIColor uint → type ANSIColor = ansi.IndexedColor.
type AdaptiveColor → compat.AdaptiveColor or LightDark.
type CompleteColor → compat.CompleteColor or Complete.
type CompleteAdaptiveColor → compat.CompleteAdaptiveColor.
WithWhitespaceForeground(c)/WithWhitespaceBackground(c) → WithWhitespaceStyle(s).
renderer.NewStyle() → lipgloss.NewStyle().
```

  or pre-resolved style fields the same way it does today. Inspect theme.go's current public API/shape
  before changing it, and preserve it as much as possible; only touch what the v1→v2 diff forces you to touch.
- `component`/`modal`/`pane` packages must NEVER import the root `tui` package (no import cycles). Do not
  introduce a cycle to work around a v2 API change.
- Pickers (branch_picker.go, directory_picker.go) and colour/key editors in modal/config.go are bespoke —
  do NOT refactor them onto `bubbles/list`. Port them as-is onto the v2 API, preserving their exact current
  UX/behavior.
- No narrative inline comments; only non-obvious constraints. Exported types/funcs need doc comments
  (revive lint enforces this) — don't remove existing doc comments.
- Package docs live in doc.go files only — don't move them.
- This is a **pure migration**: don't change behavior, don't refactor unrelated code, don't fix unrelated
  pre-existing issues.

## Workflow — iterate methodically

1. First, read AGENTS.md at the repo root and internal/tui/theme/theme.go in full to understand the current
   theme API shape (Theme struct, exported style fields, color constants) since that's the leaf you must
   preserve the external shape of.
2. Update go.mod/go.sum: `go get charm.land/bubbletea/v2@latest charm.land/bubbles/v2@latest charm.land/lipgloss/v2@latest && go mod tidy`.
3. Migrate `internal/tui/theme/theme.go` first (the leaf) — swap lipgloss import, fix Color()/TerminalColor→color.Color usages, AdaptiveColor→compat.AdaptiveColor if used, keep the same exported Theme struct/fields/methods signature wherever possible so downstream files compile unchanged against theme.Theme usage. Then fix theme_test.go.
4. Migrate `internal/tui/component/*.go` next (keymap.go and its _test.go first since keymap dispatch
   patterns — tea.KeyMsg switches — repeat across many files and this file likely defines the shared
   keymap abstraction used everywhere), then tabs.go, fuzzy.go, text.go, modal.go, branch_picker.go,
   directory_picker.go and their _test.go files.
5. Migrate `internal/tui/modal/*.go` (confirm.go, create.go, config.go) and their _test.go files.
6. Migrate `internal/tui/pane/*.go` (pane.go, preview.go, diff.go, terminal.go, tabbed.go) and pane_test.go.
7. Migrate the root `internal/tui/*.go` files: app.go (Program construction, Init/Update/View, key
   dispatch — this is the biggest and most central file), filter.go, sessionlist.go, checkpoints.go,
   checkpoint_search.go, notify.go, icons.go, and all their _test.go files.
8. After each package (or logical group) compiles, run `go build ./...` to catch cross-package breakage
   early rather than waiting until the very end.
9. Once everything compiles: run `gofmt -l .` and fix formatting; run `goimports -w .` if available
   (check with `which goimports`, it may need `make install` first — if unavailable just ensure imports
   are well-formed manually).
10. Run `go vet ./...` and fix any issues.
11. Run `golangci-lint run ./...` if available (check with `which golangci-lint`; if missing, note it
    and skip — do not attempt to install new linting tools).
12. Run `go test ./...` and fix all failures. Test files use the same tea/bubbles/lipgloss v1 APIs and
    need the same treatment — update test helpers, mock messages (tea.KeyMsg{...} literals → tea.KeyPressMsg{...}),
    and any assertions on renamed fields (msg.Type→msg.Code, msg.Runes→msg.Text, etc).
13. Run `go build -buildvcs=false -o bin/wasa ./cmd/wasa` (mirrors the Makefile's `make build` target) to
    confirm the final binary builds.
14. Finally, run `grep -rn "charmbracelet/bubbletea\|charmbracelet/bubbles\|charmbracelet/lipgloss" internal cmd`
    and confirm there are ZERO matches (aside from any go.sum comment lines, which don't matter — only
    check .go files). Also confirm go.mod no longer lists any charmbracelet bubbletea/bubbles/lipgloss
    v1 requirement (indirect deps like charmbracelet/colorprofile, charmbracelet/x/ansi, charmbracelet/x/term,
    charmbracelet/x/cellbuf are fine to keep as transitive deps if go mod tidy keeps them).

## Verification commands (must all pass before you finish)

```sh
cd /home/jcarlsson/.wasa/worktrees/0c1bb6a9d11f/task-141-upgrade-the-tui-to-new-bubble-tea-v2
go build ./...
go vet ./...
go test ./...
go build -buildvcs=false -o bin/wasa ./cmd/wasa
grep -rn "charmbracelet/bubbletea\|charmbracelet/bubbles\|charmbracelet/lipgloss" internal cmd --include=*.go
```
The grep must return no output. Do not consider the task done until build, vet, and test all pass and
that grep is empty. If `make lint` (golangci-lint) is available, run it too and fix any new issues it
flags that are directly caused by your changes (pre-existing lint issues unrelated to this migration are
out of scope).

## Final report

When done, report: (a) confirmation of go.mod's new require lines for the three modules, (b) the exact
versions resolved, (c) pass/fail status of build/vet/test/lint, (d) any behavior-preservation judgment
calls you had to make (e.g. how you handled AdaptiveColor if theme.go used it, or any place where the v1
and v2 APIs don't map 1:1 and you had to choose an approach) so they can be reviewed, and (e) a short list
of every file you touched.

1. package theme
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
5. 
6. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/config"
7. )
8. 
9. // Theme is the cockpit's resolved set of lipgloss styles. Its colours are not
10. // fixed: NewTheme rebuilds every style from a config.Theme, so the zero-config
11. // cockpit reproduces the historical palette while a config.json recolours the
12. // whole cockpit. The aesthetic, adapted from claude-squad / agent-deck: a purple
13. // accent on borders and the active tab, a green status dot for running and a dim
14. // grey one for exited, and a light selection band that flips the row text dark.
15. //
16. // The fields are exported so the root view code and the bespoke editors that
17. // hold a Theme can read them across the package boundary.
18. type Theme struct {
19. 	PaneStyle      lipgloss.Style
20. 	PaneTitleStyle lipgloss.Style
21. 
22. 	ActiveTabStyle   lipgloss.Style
23. 	InactiveTabStyle lipgloss.Style
24. 
25. 	PaneTabActiveStyle   lipgloss.Style
26. 	PaneTabInactiveStyle lipgloss.Style
27. 	PaneWindowStyle      lipgloss.Style
28. 
29. 	RunningDotStyle lipgloss.Style
30. 	WaitingDotStyle lipgloss.Style
31. 	IdleDotStyle    lipgloss.Style
32. 	ExitedDotStyle  lipgloss.Style
33. 
34. 	RowTitleStyle lipgloss.Style
35. 	RowDescStyle  lipgloss.Style
36. 
37. 	SelRowTitleStyle lipgloss.Style
38. 	SelRowDescStyle  lipgloss.Style
39. 
40. 	BannerStyle lipgloss.Style
41. 	DimStyle    lipgloss.Style
42. 	ErrorStyle  lipgloss.Style
43. 
44. 	DiffAddStyle  lipgloss.Style
45. 	DiffDelStyle  lipgloss.Style
46. 	DiffHunkStyle lipgloss.Style
47. 	DiffMetaStyle lipgloss.Style
48. 
49. 	DiffAddLineStyle lipgloss.Style
50. 	DiffDelLineStyle lipgloss.Style
51. 	DiffGutterStyle  lipgloss.Style
52. 	DiffFileStyle    lipgloss.Style
53. 
54. 	ModalStyle  lipgloss.Style
55. 	PickerStyle lipgloss.Style
56. 	MatchStyle  lipgloss.Style
57. 
58. 	BtnInactiveStyle lipgloss.Style
59. 	BtnCancelStyle   lipgloss.Style
60. 	BtnConfirmStyle  lipgloss.Style
61. 	BtnDangerStyle   lipgloss.Style
62. 
63. 	MenuKeyStyle  lipgloss.Style
64. 	MenuDescStyle lipgloss.Style
65. 	MenuSepStyle  lipgloss.Style
66. 
67. 	TitleStyle        lipgloss.Style
68. 	FocusedLabelStyle lipgloss.Style
69. 	LabelStyle        lipgloss.Style
70. }
71. 
72. // themeColor converts a config.Color to a lipgloss colour. A colour whose light
73. // and dark variants are equal becomes a plain lipgloss.Color (identical to a
74. // fixed colour); one with distinct variants becomes an AdaptiveColor that lipgloss
75. // resolves against the terminal background.
76. func themeColor(c config.Color) lipgloss.TerminalColor {
77. 	if c.Light == c.Dark {
78. 		return lipgloss.Color(c.Light)
79. 	}
80. 	return lipgloss.AdaptiveColor{Light: c.Light, Dark: c.Dark}
81. }
82. 
83. // NewTheme builds the cockpit's styles from t. New calls it with the resolved
84. // theme at startup and applyConfig calls it again when the config changes live.
85. func NewTheme(t config.Theme) Theme {
86. 	accent := themeColor(t.Accent)
87. 	running := themeColor(t.Running)
88. 	waiting := themeColor(t.Waiting)
89. 	idle := themeColor(t.Idle)
90. 	exited := themeColor(t.Exited)
91. 	title := themeColor(t.Title)
92. 	desc := themeColor(t.Desc)
93. 	selFg := themeColor(t.SelectionFg)
94. 	selBg := themeColor(t.SelectionBg)
95. 	danger := themeColor(t.Danger)
96. 	onAccent := themeColor(t.OnAccent)
97. 	inactiveBtnBg := themeColor(t.InactiveBtnBg)
98. 
99. 	var th Theme
100. 
101. 	th.PaneStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
102. 		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
103. 		BorderForeground(accent)
104. 
105. 	th.PaneTitleStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
106. 		Bold(true).
107. 		Foreground(accent).
108. 		Padding(0, 1)
109. 
110. 	th.ActiveTabStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
111. 		Bold(true).
112. 		Foreground(onAccent).
113. 		Background(accent).
114. 		Padding(0, 2)
115. 
116. 	th.InactiveTabStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
117. 		Foreground(desc).
118. 		Padding(0, 2)
119. 
120. 	th.PaneTabInactiveStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
121. 		Border(tabBorderWithBottom("┴", "─", "┴"), true).
122. 		BorderForeground(accent).
123. 		Foreground(desc).
124. 		Align(lipgloss.Center)
125. 	th.PaneTabActiveStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
126. 		Border(tabBorderWithBottom("┘", " ", "└"), true).
127. 		BorderForeground(accent).
128. 		Bold(true).
129. 		Foreground(accent).
130. 		Align(lipgloss.Center)
131. 	th.PaneWindowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
132. 		BorderForeground(accent).
133. 		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder(), false, true, true, true)
134. 
135. 	th.RunningDotStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(running)
136. 	th.WaitingDotStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(waiting)
137. 	th.IdleDotStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(idle)
138. 	th.ExitedDotStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(exited)
139. 
140. 	th.RowTitleStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(title)
141. 	th.RowDescStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(desc)
142. 
143. 	th.SelRowTitleStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
144. 		Bold(true).
145. 		Background(selBg).
146. 		Foreground(selFg)
147. 	th.SelRowDescStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
148. 		Background(selBg).
149. 		Foreground(selFg)
150. 
151. 	th.BannerStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(accent)
152. 	th.DimStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(desc)
153. 	th.ErrorStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(danger)
154. 
155. 	th.DiffAddStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(running)
156. 	th.DiffDelStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(danger)
157. 	th.DiffHunkStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(accent)
158. 	th.DiffMetaStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(desc)
159. 
160. 	addBg := themeColor(t.DiffAddBg)
161. 	delBg := themeColor(t.DiffDelBg)
162. 	th.DiffAddLineStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
163. 		Foreground(title).
164. 		Background(addBg)
165. 	th.DiffDelLineStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
166. 		Foreground(title).
167. 		Background(delBg)
168. 	th.DiffGutterStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(desc)
169. 	th.DiffFileStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(accent)
170. 
171. 	th.ModalStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
172. 		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
173. 		BorderForeground(danger).
174. 		Padding(1, 2)
175. 
176. 	th.PickerStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
177. 		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
178. 		BorderForeground(accent).
179. 		Padding(1, 2)
180. 
181. 	th.MatchStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(accent)
182. 
183. 	btnBase := lipgloss.NewStyle().Padding(0, 3)
184. 	th.BtnInactiveStyle = btnBase.
185. 		Foreground(desc).
186. 		Background(inactiveBtnBg)
187. 	th.BtnCancelStyle = btnBase.
188. 		Bold(true).
189. 		Foreground(onAccent).
190. 		Background(accent)
191. 	th.BtnConfirmStyle = th.BtnCancelStyle
192. 	th.BtnDangerStyle = btnBase.
193. 		Bold(true).
194. 		Foreground(onAccent).
195. 		Background(danger)
196. 
197. 	th.MenuKeyStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
198. 		Bold(true).
199. 		Foreground(themeColor(t.MenuKey))
200. 	th.MenuDescStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
201. 		Foreground(themeColor(t.MenuDesc))
202. 	th.MenuSepStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
203. 		Foreground(themeColor(t.MenuSep))
204. 
205. 	th.TitleStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(accent)
206. 	th.FocusedLabelStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(accent)
207. 	th.LabelStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(desc)
208. 
209. 	return th
210. }
211. 
212. // tabBorderWithBottom is a rounded border with its bottom edge overridden, used
213. // for the pane tab boxes: an inactive tab closes its bottom against the window
214. // rule with ┴ corners, while the active tab opens its bottom (blank middle, ┘ └
215. // corners) so the box flows into the content window beneath it, the way a
216. // browser tab connects to its page.
217. func tabBorderWithBottom(left, middle, right string) lipgloss.Border {
218. 	b := lipgloss.RoundedBorder()
219. 	b.BottomLeft = left
220. 	b.Bottom = middle
221. 	b.BottomRight = right
222. 	return b
223. }
224.

 // resolves against the terminal background.
 func themeColor(c config.Color) lipgloss.TerminalColor {
 	if c.Light == c.Dark {
 		return lipgloss.Color(c.Light)
 	}
 	return lipgloss.AdaptiveColor{Light: c.Light, Dark: c.Dark}
 }
 
 // NewTheme builds the cockpit's styles from t. New calls it with the resolved
 // theme at startup and applyConfig calls it again when the config changes live.
 func NewTheme(t config.Theme) Theme {
 	accent := themeColor(t.Accent)
 	running := themeColor(t.Running)
 	waiting := themeColor(t.Waiting)
 	idle := themeColor(t.Idle)
 	exited := themeColor(t.Exited)
 	title := themeColor(t.Title)
 	desc := themeColor(t.Desc)
 	selFg := themeColor(t.SelectionFg)
 	selBg := themeColor(t.SelectionBg)
 	danger := themeColor(t.Danger)
 	onAccent := themeColor(t.OnAccent)
 	inactiveBtnBg := themeColor(t.InactiveBtnBg)
 
 	var th Theme
 
 	th.PaneStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
 		BorderForeground(accent)
 
 	th.PaneTitleStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Bold(true).
 		Foreground(accent).
 		Padding(0, 1)
 
 	th.ActiveTabStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Bold(true).
 		Foreground(onAccent).
 		Background(accent).
 		Padding(0, 2)
 
 	th.InactiveTabStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Foreground(desc).
 		Padding(0, 2)
 
 	th.PaneTabInactiveStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Border(tabBorderWithBottom("┴", "─", "┴"), true).
 		BorderForeground(accent).
 		Foreground(desc).
 		Align(lipgloss.Center)
 	th.PaneTabActiveStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Border(tabBorderWithBottom("┘", " ", "└"), true).
 		BorderForeground(accent).
 		Bold(true).
 		Foreground(accent).
 		Align(lipgloss.Center)
 	th.PaneWindowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		BorderForeground(accent).
 		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder(), false, true, true, true)
 
 	th.RunningDotStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(running)
 	th.WaitingDotStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(waiting)
 	th.IdleDotStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(idle)
 	th.ExitedDotStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(exited)
 
 	th.RowTitleStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(title)
 	th.RowDescStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(desc)
 
 	th.SelRowTitleStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Bold(true).
 		Background(selBg).
 		Foreground(selFg)
 	th.SelRowDescStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Background(selBg).
 		Foreground(selFg)
 
 	th.BannerStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(accent)
 	th.DimStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(desc)
 	th.ErrorStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(danger)
 
 	th.DiffAddStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(running)
 	th.DiffDelStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(danger)
 	th.DiffHunkStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(accent)
 	th.DiffMetaStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(desc)
 
 	addBg := themeColor(t.DiffAddBg)
 	delBg := themeColor(t.DiffDelBg)
 	th.DiffAddLineStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Foreground(title).
 		Background(addBg)
 	th.DiffDelLineStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Foreground(title).
 		Background(delBg)
 	th.DiffGutterStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(desc)
 	th.DiffFileStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(accent)
 
 	th.ModalStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
 		BorderForeground(danger).
 		Padding(1, 2)
 
 	th.PickerStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
 		BorderForeground(accent).
 		Padding(1, 2)
 
 	th.MatchStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(accent)
 
 	btnBase := lipgloss.NewStyle().Padding(0, 3)
 	th.BtnInactiveStyle = btnBase.
 		Foreground(desc).
 		Background(inactiveBtnBg)
 	th.BtnCancelStyle = btnBase.
 		Bold(true).
 		Foreground(onAccent).
 		Background(accent)
 	th.BtnConfirmStyle = th.BtnCancelStyle
 	th.BtnDangerStyle = btnBase.
 		Bold(true).
 		Foreground(onAccent).
 		Background(danger)
 
 	th.MenuKeyStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Bold(true).
 		Foreground(themeColor(t.MenuKey))
 	th.MenuDescStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Foreground(themeColor(t.MenuDesc))
 	th.MenuSepStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
 		Foreground(themeColor(t.MenuSep))
 
 	th.TitleStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(accent)
 	th.FocusedLabelStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(accent)
 	th.LabelStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(desc)
 
 	return th
 }
 
 // tabBorderWithBottom is a rounded border with its bottom edge overridden, used
 // for the pane tab boxes: an inactive tab closes its bottom against the window
 // rule with ┴ corners, while the active tab opens its bottom (blank middle, ┘ └
 // corners) so the box flows into the content window beneath it, the way a
 // browser tab connects to its page.
 func tabBorderWithBottom(left, middle, right string) lipgloss.Border {
 	b := lipgloss.RoundedBorder()
 	b.BottomLeft = left
 	b.Bottom = middle
 	b.BottomRight = right
 	return b
 }

1. # AGENTS.md — wasa-cli
2. 
3. The wasa **runner**: a terminal cockpit that launches and supervises AI coding
4. agents, each in its own git worktree, kept alive in tmux, shown in a Bubble Tea
5. TUI. Standalone — the control plane is a layer you `wasa link` into, never a
6. dependency. Workspace map: `../AGENTS.md`; product: `../VISION.md`.
7. 
8. **Stack:** Go 1.26.3 · Bubble Tea + Bubbles + lipgloss (TUI) · tmux (sessions,
9. shelled out) · git (worktrees, shelled out). Linux/macOS only — the Windows
10. entry point just prints "use WSL2" and exits.
11. 
12. ## Commands
13. 
14. ```sh
15. make install   # install golangci-lint, goimports, golines
16. make build     # go build -buildvcs=false -o bin/wasa ./cmd/wasa
17. make run       # build then run
18. make fmt       # goimports -w . && golines -m 80 -w .
19. make lint      # go vet ./... && golangci-lint run ./...
20. make test      # go test ./...
21. make env       # (Windows) re-run inside WSL; put a fresh bin/wasa first on PATH
22. ```
23. 
24. **Verify a change:** get `make lint` clean, then build and drive the affected
25. flow. Anything touching tmux must be exercised inside WSL2 (a distro with tmux +
26. Go), not native Windows.
27. 
28. ## Layout
29. 
30. `internal/<name>/` dirs are subsystems. Orchestration seams each live in one
31. place so the CLI and the TUI drive the same path.
32. 
33. **Seams:**
34. 
35. - `launch/` — session create/kill orchestration (worktree → hook → bootstrap → tmux). The one create flow both CLI and TUI use.
36. - `finish/` — teardown: stop tmux, remove worktree, delete branch. Never merges/rebases/pushes — local artifacts only.
37. - `backend/` — the session-backend interface + `Default` (host selector). `backend/unix/` is the tmux impl (`//go:build !windows`).
38. - `worktree/` — git worktree porcelain (shells out to `git`).
39. - `bootstrap/` — copies/symlinks untracked-but-needed files (deps, `.env`) into a fresh worktree; assigns an isolated dev port.
40. - `hook/` — runs a profile's post-worktree hook (deps install, `.env`, cache warm).
41. - `profile/` — resolves a profile into the `KEY=VALUE` env injected at launch.
42. - `repo/` — resolves a directory to its canonical git identity → the content-addressed workspace id.
43. - `record/` — session recording: checkpoints (meta + intent + redacted transcript) on `refs/wasa/checkpoints` via git plumbing, agent hook handling, `.claude/settings.json` hook install/remove, read-back. Best-effort by contract: recording never fails a session.
44. - `registry/` — persistent repo-keyed data model (workspaces + sessions) as one JSON doc under `$WASA_HOME`; reconciles against tmux on startup.
45. - `sessionstatus/` — per-session activity state (working/waiting/idle) and how it's derived.
46. - `config/` — loads `$WASA_HOME/config.json` over defaults; owns the theme/keys/layout schema; validates at startup.
47. 
48. **Entry & UI:**
49. 
50. - `cmd/wasa/` — `main` (`//go:build !windows`) calls `cli.Run(version, args)`; `main_windows.go` is the WSL stub. `version` via `-ldflags "-X main.version=…"`.
51. - `cli/` — flag parsing, usage, subcommand dispatch.
52. - `tui/` — the cockpit (Bubble Tea): one tab per workspace, sessions with status dots, create/attach/kill. Drives the seams; never reimplements them.
53. - `tui/theme/` — resolved lipgloss styles. A leaf package (config + lipgloss only) so every layer imports `Theme` without an import cycle.
54. - `tui/component/` — generic building blocks (keymap, pickers, tab strip, overlay helpers). Knows nothing about registry/sessions/workspaces.
55. - `tui/modal/` — full-screen modals (create form, confirm, settings editor).
56. - `tui/pane/` — right-pane feature machines (live preview, git diff, companion terminal).
57. 
58. ## Hard rules
59. 
60. - **The CLI is standalone** — the web / control-plane is linked in, never a build or runtime dependency. Offline/solo must keep working exactly as today.
61. - **One seam, one place** — CLI and TUI both go through `launch`/`finish`/`repo`/`registry`; don't reimplement a create/teardown/resolve path in a caller.
62. - **TUI imports flow one way:** `theme` is a leaf; `component`/`modal`/`pane` may build on `component` but **never import the root `tui` package** (no cycles). The root wires the pieces and routes their result messages.
63. - **Pickers and colour/key editors stay bespoke** — they are not `bubbles/list`. Don't fold them into it.
64. - **Package docs go in `doc.go`** (comment + `package` clause only), never inline in a functional file:
65.   ```go
66.   // Package worktree wraps the git worktree porcelain. ...
67.   package worktree
68.   ```
69. - No narrative inline comments; only non-obvious constraints. Exported types/funcs still need a doc comment (revive enforces it).
70. 
71. ## Config & storage
72. 
73. `$WASA_HOME` holds the registry JSON and optional `config.json` (theme, keys,
74. layout), validated at startup so a typo or conflicting binding fails loudly
75. rather than silently mis-applying.
76.

result.content:
261. 	wsID    string
262. 	name    string
263. 	created bool
264. 	err     error
265. }
266. 
267. type attachedMsg struct {
268. 	sessionID string
269. 	err       error
270. }
271. 
272. // recordToggledMsg carries the outcome of toggling repo-level recording for a
273. // workspace back to the update loop. enabled says which direction the toggle
274. // went; agents is the wired tool set when it was turned on; none is set when the
275. // toggle was a no-op because no supported agent is on PATH (mirroring the
276. // `wasa record enable` behaviour, but as a transient message rather than an
277. // error).
278. type recordToggledMsg struct {
279. 	wsName  string
280. 	enabled bool
281. 	agents  []string
282. 	none    bool
283. 	err     error
284. }
285. 
286. // Update implements tea.Model.
287. func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
288. 	switch msg := msg.(type) {
289. 	case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
290. 		m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
291. 		m.sizeDiffViewport()
292. 		m.sizeCheckpoints()
293. 		return m, nil
294. 
295. 	case tickMsg:
296. 		m.sweepStatuses()
297. 		return m, tea.Batch(tick(), m.paneTick())
298. 
299. 	case churnTickMsg:
300. 		return m, tea.Batch(churnTick(), m.churnCmd(), m.refreshDiffCmd())
301. 
302. 	case churnMsg:
303. 		m.applyChurn(msg)
304. 		return m, nil
305. 
306. 	case pane.PreviewMsg:
307. 		return m, m.tabbed.Preview.Apply(msg)
308. 
309. 	case pane.TermMsg:
310. 		return m, m.applyTerm(msg)
311. 
312. 	case pane.DiffMsg:
313. 		return m, m.applyDiff(msg)
314. 
315. 	case createdMsg:
316. 		if msg.err != nil {
317. 			m.err = msg.err
318. 			return m, nil
319. 		}
320. 		m.err = nil
321. 		if msg.session.Branch != "" {
322. 			m.status = "created session on " + msg.session.Branch
323. 		} else {
324. 			m.status = "created session in " + msg.session.WorkingDir
325. 		}
326. 		return m, m.refresh()
327. 
328. 	case killedMsg:
329. 		if msg.err != nil {
330. 			m.err = msg.err
331. 			return m, nil
332. 		}
333. 		m.err = nil
334. 		m.status = "killed session"
335. 		return m, m.refresh()
336. 
337. 	case deletedMsg:
338. 		if msg.err != nil {
339. 			m.err = msg.err
340. 			return m, nil
341. 		}
342. 		m.err = nil
343. 		m.status = "deleted session"
344. 		return m, m.refresh()
345. 
346. 	case pausedMsg:
347. 		if msg.err != nil {
348. 			if msg.retry != nil {
349. 				return m.enterConfirmForcePause(msg.retry, msg.err)
350. 			}
351. 			m.err = msg.err
352. 			return m, nil
353. 		}
354. 		m.err = nil
355. 		m.status = "paused session — branch kept, worktree freed"
356. 		return m, m.refresh()
357. 
358. 	case resumedMsg:
359. 		if msg.err != nil {
360. 			m.err = msg.err
361. 			return m, nil
362. 		}
363. 		m.err = nil
364. 		m.status = "resumed session"
365. 		return m, m.refresh()
366. 
367. 	case workspaceDeletedMsg:
368. 		if msg.err != nil {
369. 			m.err = msg.err
370. 			return m, nil
371. 		}
372. 		m.err = nil
373. 		m.status = "deleted workspace " + msg.name
374. 		return m, m.refresh()
375. 
376. 	case recordToggledMsg:
377. 		if msg.err != nil {
378. 			m.err = msg.err
379. 			return m, nil
380. 		}
381. 		m.err = nil
382. 		switch {
383. 		case msg.none:
384. 			m.status = "no supported agents found on PATH"
385. 		case msg.enabled:
386. 			m.status = "recording enabled for " + msg.wsName +
387. 				" (" + strings.Join(msg.agents, ", ") + ")"
388. 		default:
389. 			m.status = "recording disabled for " + msg.wsName
390. 		}
391. 		return m, m.refresh()
392. 
393. 	case workspaceAddedMsg:
394. 		if msg.err != nil {
395. 			m.err = msg.err
396. 			return m, m.afterListChange()
397. 		}
398. 		m.err = nil
399. 		if msg.created {
400. 			m.status = "initialized and added workspace " + msg.name
401. 		} else {
402. 			m.status = "workspace " + msg.name + " already added"
403. 		}
404. 		m.activeID = msg.wsID
405. 		m.cursor = 0
406. 		return m, m.refresh()
407. 
408. 	case attachedMsg:
409. 		if msg.err != nil {
410. 			m.err = msg.err
411. 			return m, nil
412. 		}
413. 		m.reg.MarkAttached(msg.sessionID)
414. 		if err := m.reg.Save(); err != nil {
415. 			m.err = err
416. 			return m, nil
417. 		}
418. 		return m, m.refresh()
419. 
420. 	case component.FilterTickMsg:
421. 		if m.mode == modePick || m.mode == modePickWorkspace {
422. 			return m, m.picker.TickFilter(msg.Gen)
423. 		}
424. 		return m, nil
425. 
426. 	case component.FilterResultMsg:
427. 		if m.mode == modePick || m.mode == modePickWorkspace {
428. 			m.picker.ApplyFilterResult(msg)
429. 		}
430. 		return m, nil
431. 
432. 	case ckptSearchTickMsg:
433. 		if m.mode == modeCheckpointSearch {
434. 			return m.checkpointSearchTick(msg.gen)
435. 		}
436. 		return m, nil
437. 
438. 	case ckptSearchResultMsg:
439. 		if m.mode == modeCheckpointSearch {
440. 			return m.applyCheckpointSearchResult(msg)
441. 		}
442. 		return m, nil
443. 
444. 	case modal.ConfirmAcceptedMsg:
445. 		cmd := m.confirmCmd
446. 		m.mode = modeList
447. 		m.confirmCmd = nil
448. 		return m, cmd
449. 
450. 	case modal.ConfirmCancelledMsg:
451. 		m.mode = modeList
452. 		m.confirmCmd = nil
453. 		return m, nil
454. 
455. 	case modal.FormSubmitMsg:
456. 		return m.submitCreate()
457. 
458. 	case modal.FormCancelMsg:
459. 		m.mode = modeList
460. 		return m, nil
461. 
462. 	case modal.FormPickDirMsg:
463. 		return m.enterPick()
464. 
465. 	case modal.FormPickBranchMsg:
466. 		return m.enterBranchPick()
467. 
468. 	case component.DirChosenMsg:
469. 		if m.mode == modePickWorkspace {
470. 			return m.addWorkspace(msg.Path)
471. 		}
472. 		m.form.SetDir(msg.Path)
473. 		m.form.SetProfiles(m.profilesFor(m.form.BranchRepo))
474. 		m.mode = modeCreate
475. 		return m, textinput.Blink
476. 
477. 	case component.DirCancelledMsg:
478. 		if m.mode == modePickWorkspace {
479. 			m.mode = modeList
480. 			return m, m.afterListChange()
481. 		}
482. 		m.mode = modeCreate
483. 		return m, textinput.Blink
484. 
485. 	case component.BranchChosenMsg:
486. 		m.form.SetBranch(msg.Branch)
487. 		m.mode = modeCreate
488. 		return m, textinput.Blink
489. 
490. 	case component.BranchCancelledMsg:
491. 		m.mode = modeCreate
492. 		return m, textinput.Blink
493. 
494. 	case modal.ConfigApplyMsg:
495. 		return m.applyConfig(m.editor.Config())
496. 
497. 	case modal.ConfigCloseMsg:
498. 		m.mode = modeList
499. 		return m, m.tabbed.Preview.SetTarget(m.previewTarget())
500. 	}
501. 
502. 	switch m.mode {
503. 	case modeCreate:
504. 		return m.updateCreate(msg)
505. 	case modeConfirm:
506. 		return m.updateConfirm(msg)
507. 	case modePick, modePickWorkspace:
508. 		return m.updatePick(msg)
509. 	case modePickBranch:
510. 		return m.updateBranchPick(msg)
511. 	case modeConfig:
512. 		return m.updateConfig(msg)
513. 	case modeCheckpoints:
514. 		return m.updateCheckpoints(msg)
515. 	case modeCheckpointSearch:
516. 		return m.updateCheckpointSearch(msg)
517. 	}
518. 	return m.updateList(msg)
519. }
520.

1. package tui
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"fmt"
5. 	"os"
6. 	"strings"
7. 	"sync"
8. 	"time"
9. 
10. 	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
11. 	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
12. 
13. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/backend"
14. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/config"
15. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/launch"
16. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/record"
17. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/registry"
18. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/repo"
19. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/sessionstatus"
20. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/tui/component"
21. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/tui/modal"
22. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/tui/pane"
23. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/tui/theme"
24. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/worktree"
25. )
26. 
27. // mode is the model's interaction mode: browsing the session list or filling in
28. // the create form.
29. type mode int
30. 
31. const (
32. 	modeList mode = iota
33. 	modeCreate
34. 	modeConfirm
35. 	modePick
36. 	modePickWorkspace
37. 	modePickBranch
38. 	modeConfig
39. 	modeCheckpoints
40. 	modeCheckpointSearch
41. )
42. 
43. // Model is the cockpit's Bubble Tea model. It holds the registry it drives, the
44. // most-recently-used workspaces snapshot, the active workspace (tracked by id so
45. // it follows the workspace when an attach or create reorders the tabs), and the
46. // list cursor. home is $WASA_HOME, the data directory sessions are launched
47. // against; osHome is the user's home directory, used only to root and abbreviate
48. // the directory browser — the two are distinct and must not be conflated.
49. //
50. // The three right-pane feature machines — preview, diff and terminal — own their
51. // own state and lifecycle (see internal/tui/pane). The Model holds them through
52. // the pane.Tabbed container, which also tracks the active tab: the Model targets
53. // each at the selected session, routes the typed messages back, and reaches the
54. // panes through m.tabbed to render the active tab's body.
55. type Model struct {
56. 	home   string
57. 	osHome string
58. 	reg    *registry.Registry
59. 	tmux   backend.SessionBackend
60. 	stream backend.StreamingBackend
61. 	cfg    config.Config
62. 	keys   component.Keymap
63. 	theme  theme.Theme
64. 
65. 	workspaces []*registry.Workspace
66. 	activeID   string
67. 	cursor     int
68. 
69. 	mode    mode
70. 	form    modal.CreateForm
71. 	confirm modal.ConfirmDialog
72. 	picker  component.DirectoryPicker
