120. // New builds a cockpit model over reg. currentID is the workspace for the
121. // repository wasa was launched in; it becomes the initially active tab when
122. // present, otherwise the most-recently-used workspace is. cfg is the resolved
123. // cockpit configuration (theme, keys, layout); pass config.Default for the
124. // built-in behaviour.
125. func New(
126. 	home string,
127. 	reg *registry.Registry,
128. 	currentID string,
129. 	cfg config.Config,
130. ) Model {
131. 	be := backend.Default()
132. 	th := theme.NewTheme(cfg.Theme)
133. 	m := Model{
134. 		home:         home,
135. 		reg:          reg,
136. 		tmux:         be,
137. 		cfg:          cfg,
138. 		keys:         component.NewKeymap(cfg.Keys),
139. 		theme:        th,
140. 		now:          time.Now,
141. 		statuses:     sessionstatus.NewTracker(time.Now),
142. 		notify:       makeNotifier(cfg.Notify),
143. 		lastNotifyAt: make(map[string]time.Time),
144. 		lastStatus:   make(map[string]sessionstatus.Status),
145. 		churn:        make(map[string]churnStat),
146. 		recording:    make(map[string][]string),
147. 		recorded:     make(map[string]record.Entry),
148. 	}
149. 	m.osHome, _ = os.UserHomeDir()
150. 	if s, ok := be.(backend.StreamingBackend); ok {
151. 		m.stream = s
152. 	}
153. 	m.tabbed = pane.NewTabbed(m.stream, be, th)
154. 	m.workspaces = reg.ListWorkspaces()
155. 	switch {
156. 	case currentID != "" && m.hasWorkspace(currentID):
157. 		m.activeID = currentID
158. 	case len(m.workspaces) > 0:
159. 		m.activeID = m.workspaces[0].ID
160. 	}
161. 	m.refreshRecording()
162. 	return m
163. }
164. 
165. // Run launches the cockpit over reg and blocks until the user quits. It uses the
166. // alternate screen so the terminal is restored on exit and around every attach.
167. // cfg is the resolved cockpit configuration.
168. func Run(
169. 	home string,
170. 	reg *registry.Registry,
171. 	currentID string,
172. 	cfg config.Config,
173. ) error {
174. 	_, err := tea.NewProgram(
175. 		New(home, reg, currentID, cfg), tea.WithAltScreen(),
176. 	).Run()
177. 	return err
178. }
179. 
180. // previewInterval is the cadence of the preview fallback tick. On the streaming
181. // (unix/tmux) path the live control-mode connection drives updates and this tick
182. // is a near no-op that only reconnects a dropped stream; the old per-tick
183. // capture-pane subprocess is gone. On a non-streaming backend (Windows) the tick
184. // is still the poll that re-captures the selected session, preserving the prior
185. // behaviour. The list's running/exited status always comes from the registry,
186. // never from this capture.
187. const previewInterval = 750 * time.Millisecond
188. 
189. // churnInterval is the cadence of the diff-refresh tick: the selected worktree
190. // session's full diff is recomputed, and every worktree session's +N/−M churn
191. // stat is refreshed, so the pane and the list rows track the agent's edits
192. // without the user re-selecting the row. It is deliberately slower than the
193. // preview tick — numstat is cheap but still shells out to git per session — and
194. // is a no-op (no git) when there are no worktree sessions.
195. const churnInterval = time.Second
196. 
197. // Init implements tea.Model. It fires one immediate tick of each loop so the
198. // preview opens its stream (or polls) and the churn stats populate right away
199. // rather than after the first interval.
200. func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
201. 	return tea.Batch(
202. 		func() tea.Msg { return tickMsg{} },
203. 		func() tea.Msg { return churnTickMsg{} },
204. 	)
205. }
206. 
207. type tickMsg struct{}
208. 
209. func tick() tea.Cmd {
210. 	return tea.Tick(
211. 		previewInterval,
212. 		func(time.Time) tea.Msg { return tickMsg{} },
213. 	)
214. }
215. 
216. // churnTickMsg fires the diff-refresh loop; churnMsg carries the computed
217. // per-session churn back to the model. They are distinct so the timer re-arms
218. // and issues the git work in one step while the result lands in another.
219. type churnTickMsg struct{}
220. 
221. type churnMsg struct {
222. 	stats map[string]churnStat
223. }
224. 
225. func churnTick() tea.Cmd {
226. 	return tea.Tick(
227. 		churnInterval,
228. 		func(time.Time) tea.Msg { return churnTickMsg{} },
229. 	)
230. }

1. package component
2. 
3. import "github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/config"
4. 
5. // Keymap resolves a key press to a cockpit action and reports the effective key
6. // for an action so the menu can render the binding actually in force. It is built
7. // once from the resolved config, so a remapped binding drives both key handling
8. // and the displayed hints. Duplicate bindings are rejected earlier, in
9. // config.validate, so the key→action map here is unambiguous.
10. type Keymap struct {
11. 	byKey map[string]string
12. 	keys  config.Keys
13. }
14. 
15. // NewKeymap builds a Keymap from the resolved key bindings, inverting the
16. // action→keys map into the key→action lookup used at key-press time.
17. func NewKeymap(keys config.Keys) Keymap {
18. 	byKey := make(map[string]string, len(keys))
19. 	for action, ks := range keys {
20. 		for _, k := range ks {
21. 			byKey[k] = action
22. 		}
23. 	}
24. 	return Keymap{byKey: byKey, keys: keys}
25. }
26. 
27. // Action returns the action bound to key, or "" when key is unbound.
28. func (km Keymap) Action(key string) string { return km.byKey[key] }
29. 
30. // Primary returns the representative key for an action — the first one bound to
31. // it — used to label the action in the menu. It is "" when the action is unbound.
32. func (km Keymap) Primary(action string) string {
33. 	if ks := km.keys[action]; len(ks) > 0 {
34. 		return ks[0]
35. 	}
36. 	return ""
37. }
38. 
39. // KeyLabel renders a key string as the glyph shown in the menu bar. Known keys
40. // get their conventional symbol (↵, ⇥, the arrows); a ctrl chord shows as ^x; any
41. // other key is shown verbatim.
42. func KeyLabel(key string) string {
43. 	switch key {
44. 	case "":
45. 		return ""
46. 	case "enter":
47. 		return "↵"
48. 	case "tab":
49. 		return "⇥"
50. 	case "shift+tab":
51. 		return "⇧⇥"
52. 	case "up":
53. 		return "↑"
54. 	case "down":
55. 		return "↓"
56. 	case "left":
57. 		return "←"
58. 	case "right":
59. 		return "→"
60. 	case "esc":
61. 		return "esc"
62. 	case "space":
63. 		return "␣"
64. 	}
65. 	if len(key) > 5 && key[:5] == "ctrl+" {
66. 		return "^" + key[5:]
67. 	}
68. 	return key
69. }
70.

1. package tui
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"path/filepath"
5. 	"strings"
6. 
7. 	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
8. 	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9. 
10. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/registry"
11. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/tui/component"
12. )
13. 
14. // The filter's status tokens. A leading "running", "exited" or "paused" word
15. // narrows the list to that liveness state; the rest of the query is fuzzy text.
16. // These are the only three tokens — the richer activity states (waiting/idle)
17. // depend on the live-status work and are not invented here.
18. const (
19. 	tokenRunning = "running"
20. 	tokenExited  = "exited"
21. 	tokenPaused  = "paused"
22. )
23. 
24. // filterState is the cockpit's transient session filter: a one-line fuzzy query
25. // over the active workspace's sessions. It is a view, never a mutation — the
26. // registry is untouched; sessions() narrows what every list operation reads while
27. // active is set, and esc tears it down.
28. type filterState struct {
29. 	active bool
30. 	input  textinput.Model
31. }
32. 
33. // parseFilterQuery splits a raw filter query into an optional leading status
34. // token and the remaining fuzzy text. The first whitespace-delimited word, when
35. // it is exactly "running", "exited" or "paused", selects that liveness state
36. // and the rest is the fuzzy text; otherwise the whole query is fuzzy text and
37. // no status token applies. Matching is case-insensitive.
38. func parseFilterQuery(raw string) (status, text string) {
39. 	raw = strings.TrimSpace(raw)
40. 	first, rest, _ := strings.Cut(raw, " ")
41. 	switch strings.ToLower(first) {
42. 	case tokenRunning, tokenExited, tokenPaused:
43. 		return strings.ToLower(first), strings.TrimSpace(rest)
44. 	default:
45. 		return "", raw
46. 	}
47. }
48. 
49. // sessionHaystack is the text the fuzzy filter matches a session against: its
50. // display title, ref (branch, or working-dir basename for a plain session) and
51. // working-directory basename, joined so a query can hit any of them.
52. func sessionHaystack(s *registry.Session) string {
53. 	title, ref := sessionLabel(s)
54. 	return title + " " + ref + " " + filepath.Base(s.WorkingDir)
55. }
56. 
57. // matchesFilter reports whether s passes a parsed filter: the status token,
58. // when set, must match its liveness exactly — paused is its own state, so
59. // "exited" no longer means "anything not running" — and the fuzzy text, when
60. // set, must be a subsequence of its haystack. An all-empty filter matches every
61. // session.
62. func matchesFilter(s *registry.Session, status, text string) bool {
63. 	switch status {
64. 	case tokenRunning:
65. 		if s.Status != registry.StatusRunning {
66. 			return false
67. 		}
68. 	case tokenExited:
69. 		if s.Status != registry.StatusExited {
70. 			return false
71. 		}
72. 	case tokenPaused:
73. 		if s.Status != registry.StatusPaused {
74. 			return false
75. 		}
76. 	}
77. 	if text == "" {
78. 		return true
79. 	}
80. 	_, _, ok := component.FuzzyScore(text, sessionHaystack(s))
81. 	return ok
82. }
83. 
84. // enterFilter opens the session filter over the list, focusing a fresh one-line
85. // query input and resetting the cursor to the first match. It is a no-op when the
86. // active workspace has no sessions to filter.
87. func (m Model) enterFilter() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
88. 	if len(m.workspaceSessions()) == 0 {
89. 		return m, nil
90. 	}
91. 	in := textinput.New()
92. 	in.Prompt = "> "
93. 	in.Placeholder = "filter — prefix running/exited/paused"
94. 	in.CharLimit = 200
95. 	in.Width = max(m.listColWidth()-4, 10)
96. 	in.Focus()
97. 
98. 	m.filter = filterState{active: true, input: in}
99. 	m.cursor = 0
100. 	return m, textinput.Blink
101. }
102. 
103. // exitFilter tears the filter down and restores the full list with the cursor on
104. // the first session. It re-targets the preview at whatever is now selected.
105. func (m Model) exitFilter() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
106. 	m.filter = filterState{}
107. 	m.cursor = 0
108. 	return m, m.afterListChange()
109. }
110. 
111. // updateFilter routes input while the filter is open. esc closes it; enter
112. // attaches to the highlighted (filtered) session as in the list; up/down move the
113. // cursor within the filtered set; ctrl+c still quits. Every other key edits the
114. // query, after which the cursor is clamped into the narrowed set and the preview
115. // re-targeted so the live view tracks the new selection.
116. func (m Model) updateFilter(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
117. 	key, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg)
118. 	if !ok {
119. 		var cmd tea.Cmd
120. 		m.filter.input, cmd = m.filter.input.Update(msg)
121. 		return m, cmd
122. 	}
123. 
124. 	switch key.String() {
125. 	case "esc":
126. 		return m.exitFilter()
127. 	case "ctrl+c":
128. 		m.tabbed.Preview.Close()
129. 		m.tabbed.Terminal.Close(m.tmux)
130. 		return m, tea.Quit
131. 	case "enter":
132. 		return m.attach()
133. 	case "up", "ctrl+p":
134. 		if m.cursor > 0 {
135. 			m.cursor--
136. 		}
137. 		return m, m.afterListChange()
138. 	case "down", "ctrl+n":
139. 		if m.cursor < len(m.sessions())-1 {
140. 			m.cursor++
141. 		}
142. 		return m, m.afterListChange()
143. 	}
144. 
145. 	var cmd tea.Cmd
146. 	m.filter.input, cmd = m.filter.input.Update(msg)
147. 	m.clampCursor()
148. 	return m, tea.Batch(cmd, m.afterListChange())
149. }
150. 
151. // clampCursor pins the cursor into the current (possibly filtered) session set,
152. // landing it on a real row after the set shrinks under it.
153. func (m *Model) clampCursor() {
154. 	if n := len(m.sessions()); m.cursor >= n {
155. 		m.cursor = n - 1
156. 	}
157. 	m.cursor = max(m.cursor, 0)
158. }
159.

result.content:
1. package component
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"os"
5. 	"path/filepath"
6. 	"sort"
7. 	"strconv"
8. 	"strings"
9. 	"time"
10. 
11. 	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
12. 	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
13. 	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
14. 	"github.com/charmbracelet/x/ansi"
15. 
16. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/tui/theme"
17. )
18. 
19. // maxFilterDepth bounds how deep a fuzzy filter walks below the root and
20. // filterCap bounds how many matches it collects, so a filter over a large tree
21. // stays bounded. filterDebounce is how long after the last keystroke the filter
22. // walk is deferred, so typing fast spawns one walk rather than one per keystroke.
23. const (
24. 	maxFilterDepth = 6
25. 	filterCap      = 4000
26. 	filterDebounce = 150 * time.Millisecond
27. )
28. 
29. const (
30. 	focusTree = iota
31. 	focusRecent
32. )
33. 
34. // scanSkip is the set of directory names the browser never lists: dependency and
35. // build caches that are large, deep and never themselves a directory you want to
36. // start a session in. Hidden directories (those starting with a dot) are skipped
37. // separately.
38. var scanSkip = map[string]bool{
39. 	"node_modules": true,
40. 	"Library":      true,
41. 	"vendor":       true,
42. 	"target":       true,
43. 	"dist":         true,
44. 	"build":        true,
45. 	"Pods":         true,
46. }
47. 
48. // treeNode is one directory in the browser's tree. In browse mode children are
49. // loaded lazily on first expand: until then loaded is false and the node draws
50. // as collapsible. isLeaf is set once loaded with no sub-directories; isRepo
51. // marks a directory holding a .git. matched and positions are set only on the
52. // filtered tree, marking a fuzzy hit and the characters of its name that matched.
53. type treeNode struct {
54. 	path      string
55. 	name      string
56. 	parent    *treeNode
57. 	children  []*treeNode
58. 	expanded  bool
59. 	loaded    bool
60. 	isLeaf    bool
61. 	isRepo    bool
62. 	matched   bool
63. 	positions []int
64. }
65. 
66. // visRow is a node placed in the flattened, on-screen order with the indentation
67. // depth it is drawn at.
68. type visRow struct {
69. 	node  *treeNode
70. 	depth int
71. }
72. 
73. // RecentDir is one entry in the picker's recent pane: a directory drawn from
74. // session and workspace history, with its home-relative display form.
75. type RecentDir struct {
76. 	Path    string
77. 	Display string
78. }
79. 
80. // DirChosenMsg is emitted by a DirectoryPicker when the user picks a directory;
81. // Path is the chosen directory.
82. type DirChosenMsg struct{ Path string }
83. 
84. // DirCancelledMsg is emitted by a DirectoryPicker when the user dismisses it
85. // without a choice.
86. type DirCancelledMsg struct{}
87. 
88. // FilterTickMsg fires after the debounce interval to start a deferred filter
89. // walk; gen identifies the keystroke that scheduled it so a superseded tick is
90. // ignored. FilterResultMsg carries a completed filter walk back to the picker.
91. type FilterTickMsg struct{ Gen int }
92. 
93. // FilterResultMsg carries a completed filter walk back to the picker, tagged
94. // with the generation that requested it so a superseded result is ignored.
95. type FilterResultMsg struct {
96. 	gen   int
97. 	root  *treeNode
98. 	count int
99. }
100. 
101. // DirectoryPicker is the two-pane directory browser shown over the create form.
102. // The left pane is a lazily-loaded tree rooted at root that you drill into and
103. // roam upward through, and that typing fuzzy-filters broot-style; the right pane
104. // is a recent-directories quick list. tab moves focus between panes. The fuzzy
105. // filter runs off the update goroutine and debounced: a query keystroke bumps
106. // filterGen and schedules a FilterTickMsg, the tick spawns the walk, and a
107. // FilterResultMsg is applied only while its gen is still current.
108. type DirectoryPicker struct {
109. 	theme      theme.Theme
110. 	root       *treeNode
111. 	filterRoot *treeNode
112. 	query      textinput.Model
113. 	visible    []visRow
114. 	cursor     int
115. 	offset     int
116. 
117. 	recents      []RecentDir
118. 	recentCursor int
119. 	focus        int
120. 
121. 	// creating is the new-folder sub-mode: name is the folder-name prompt shown
122. 	// in place of the filter, createBase is the directory the folder is created
123. 	// under (the highlighted node when the sub-mode was entered), and createErr
124. 	// carries a failed mkdir back to the prompt. The sub-mode lets a brand-new
125. 	// project directory be made without leaving the picker for a shell.
126. 	creating   bool
127. 	name       textinput.Model
128. 	createBase string
129. 	createErr  string
130. 
131. 	width  int
132. 	height int
133. 	home   string
134. 
135. 	// filterBase is the directory the fuzzy filter searches under. The browse tree
136. 	// is rooted at the filesystem root so the whole ancestor chain is reachable,
137. 	// but the filter stays anchored to the directory the picker opened on, so
138. 	// typing a query scans that subtree rather than the entire filesystem.
139. 	filterBase string
140. 
141. 	// Chosen is the path the picker carried in its last DirChosenMsg.
142. 	Chosen string
143. 
144. 	filtering    bool
145. 	pending      bool
146. 	pendingQuery string
147. 	filterGen    int
148. 	matchCount   int
149. }
150. 
151. // NewDirectoryPicker builds the browser with the whole ancestor chain above
152. // rootPath revealed up to the filesystem root, so the tree shows where you are in
153. // the larger filesystem and the arrow keys roam up through parents and their
154. // siblings as naturally as they roam down. The cursor starts on the active
155. // directory — selectPath when given, otherwise rootPath. recents seeds the recent
156. // pane; with none the picker shows the tree alone.
157. func NewDirectoryPicker(
158. 	theme theme.Theme,
159. 	rootPath, selectPath, home string,
160. 	recents []RecentDir,
161. 	width, height int,
162. ) DirectoryPicker {
163. 	rootPath = resolvePath(rootPath)
164. 	if selectPath != "" {
165. 		selectPath = resolvePath(selectPath)
166. 	}
167. 
168. 	q := textinput.New()
169. 	q.Prompt = "> "
170. 	q.Placeholder = "type to fuzzy-filter"
171. 	q.CharLimit = 200
172. 	q.Focus()
173. 	if width > 6 {
174. 		q.Width = width - 4
175. 	}
176. 
177. 	name := textinput.New()
178. 	name.Prompt = "> "
179. 	name.Placeholder = "new folder name"
180. 	name.CharLimit = 200
181. 	if width > 6 {
182. 		name.Width = width - 4
183. 	}
184. 
185. 	root := &treeNode{
186. 		path:     rootPath,
187. 		name:     filepath.Base(rootPath),
188. 		expanded: true,
189. 		isRepo:   isRepoDir(rootPath),
190. 	}
191. 	loadChildren(root)
192. 
193. 	p := DirectoryPicker{
194. 		theme:      theme,
195. 		root:       root,
196. 		query:      q,
197. 		name:       name,
198. 		recents:    recents,
199. 		home:       home,
200. 		width:      width,
201. 		height:     height,
202. 		filterBase: rootPath,
203. 	}
204. 	p.revealToFilesystemRoot()
205. 	target := selectPath
206. 	if target == "" {
207. 		target = rootPath
208. 	}
209. 	p.rebuild()
210. 	p.cursorToPath(target)
211. 	return p
212. }
213. 
214. // resolvePath canonicalizes a path through its symlinks, because the tree
215. // lists only real directories: a symlinked path (macOS's /var →
216. // /private/var, a linked project dir) could otherwise never be revealed or
217. // selected.
218. func resolvePath(p string) string {
219. 	if resolved, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(p); err == nil {
220. 		return resolved
221. 	}
222. 	return p
223. }
224. 
225. // revealToFilesystemRoot re-roots the tree upward one level at a time until it is
226. // rooted at the filesystem root, leaving the whole ancestor chain expanded and the
227. // original directory grafted in at its true depth. Each ascendRoot loads only the
228. // children of the level it climbs to, so revealing the chain is a handful of
229. // directory reads, not a recursive walk.
230. func (p *DirectoryPicker) revealToFilesystemRoot() {
231. 	for {
232. 		parent := filepath.Dir(p.root.path)
233. 		if parent == p.root.path {
234. 			return
235. 		}
236. 		p.ascendRoot()
237. 	}
238. }
239. 
240. // Update handles a key message, returning the updated picker and a command. The
241. // command emits a DirChosenMsg when a directory is picked or a DirCancelledMsg
242. // when the picker is dismissed; on a query keystroke it carries the debounced
243. // filter tick, and otherwise it is nil.
244. func (p DirectoryPicker) Update(msg tea.Msg) (DirectoryPicker, tea.Cmd) {
245. 	key, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg)
246. 	if !ok {
247. 		return p, nil
248. 	}
249. 
250. 	if p.creating {
251. 		return p.updateCreating(key)
252. 	}
253. 
254. 	switch key.String() {
255. 	case "esc":
256. 		if p.query.Value() != "" {
257. 			p.query.SetValue("")
258. 			return p, p.onQueryChange()
259. 		}
260. 		return p, dirCancelled
261. 	case "tab":
262. 		if len(p.recents) > 0 {
263. 			p.focus = focusTree + focusRecent - p.focus
264. 		}
265. 		return p, nil
266. 	case "enter":
267. 		if path, ok := p.currentPath(); ok {
268. 			p.Chosen = path
269. 			return p, dirChosen(path)
270. 		}
271. 		return p, nil
272. 	case "up", "ctrl+p":
273. 		p.moveCursor(-1)
274. 		return p, nil
275. 	case "down", "ctrl+n":
276. 		p.moveCursor(1)
277. 		return p, nil
278. 	}
279. 
280. 	if p.focus == focusTree {
281. 		switch key.String() {
282. 		case "right":
283. 			if !p.filtering {
284. 				p.toggle()
285. 				return p, nil
286. 			}
287. 		case "left":
288. 			if !p.filtering {
289. 				p.collapseOrParent()
290. 				return p, nil
291. 			}
292. 		case "+":
293. 			if !p.filtering {
294. 				return p.beginCreate(), nil
295. 			}
296. 		}
297. 	} else {
298. 		switch key.String() {
299. 		case "left", "right", "-":
300. 			return p, nil
301. 		}
302. 	}
303. 
304. 	p.focus = focusTree
305. 	var cmd tea.Cmd
306. 	p.query, cmd = p.query.Update(msg)
307. 	return p, tea.Batch(cmd, p.onQueryChange())
308. }
309. 
310. // beginCreate enters the new-folder sub-mode, anchoring the folder-to-be under
311. // the highlighted tree directory (or the root when nothing is selected) and moving
312. // focus to the name prompt. It is reached only from the tree pane, where the
313. // selection is always a directory, so createBase is always a valid parent.
314. func (p DirectoryPicker) beginCreate() DirectoryPicker {
315. 	base, ok := p.currentPath()
316. 	if !ok {
317. 		base = p.root.path
318. 	}
319. 	p.createBase = base
320. 	p.creating = true
321. 	p.createErr = ""
322. 	p.name.SetValue("")
323. 	p.name.Focus()
324. 	p.query.Blur()
325. 	return p
326. }
327. 
328. // updateCreating handles a keystroke while the new-folder prompt is open. Enter
329. // makes the directory under createBase and picks it (so a brand-new project is
330. // created and chosen in one step); esc backs out to browsing; anything else edits
331. // the name. A mkdir failure is surfaced on the prompt rather than dismissing it,
332. // so a bad name can be corrected in place.
333. func (p DirectoryPicker) updateCreating(
334. 	key tea.KeyMsg,
335. ) (DirectoryPicker, tea.Cmd) {
336. 	switch key.String() {
337. 	case "esc":
338. 		p.creating = false
339. 		p.createErr = ""
340. 		p.name.SetValue("")
341. 		p.name.Blur()
342. 		p.query.Focus()
343. 		return p, nil
344. 	case "enter":
345. 		name := strings.TrimSpace(p.name.Value())
346. 		if name == "" {
347. 			p.createErr = "name required"
348. 			return p, nil
349. 		}
350. 		full := filepath.Join(p.createBase, name)
351. 		if err := os.MkdirAll(full, 0o755); err != nil {
352. 			p.createErr = err.Error()
353. 			return p, nil
354. 		}
355. 		p.creating = false
356. 		p.createErr = ""
357. 		p.name.SetValue("")
358. 		p.name.Blur()
359. 		p.Chosen = full
360. 		return p, dirChosen(full)
361. 	}
362. 	var cmd tea.Cmd
363. 	p.name, cmd = p.name.Update(key)
364. 	return p, cmd
365. }
366. 
367. func dirChosen(path string) tea.Cmd {
368. 	return func() tea.Msg { return DirChosenMsg{Path: path} }
369. }
370. 
371. func dirCancelled() tea.Msg { return DirCancelledMsg{} }
372. 
373. // onQueryChange reacts to an edited query: it reverts to the browse tree when the
374. // query is empty, otherwise bumps the filter generation and schedules a debounced
375. // walk, returning the tick command that will start it.
376. func (p *DirectoryPicker) onQueryChange() tea.Cmd {
377. 	p.filterGen++
378. 	q := strings.TrimSpace(p.query.Value())
379. 	if q == "" {
380. 		p.filtering = false
381. 		p.pending = false
382. 		p.filterRoot = nil
383. 		p.matchCount = 0
384. 		p.rebuild()
385. 		return nil
386. 	}
387. 	p.filtering = true
388. 	p.pending = true
389. 	p.pendingQuery = q
390. 	return filterTick(p.filterGen)
391. }
392. 
393. // TickFilter starts the deferred filter walk if the tick's generation is still
394. // the current one; a superseded tick (the user kept typing) is dropped.
395. func (p *DirectoryPicker) TickFilter(gen int) tea.Cmd {
396. 	if gen != p.filterGen || !p.pending {
397. 		return nil
398. 	}
399. 	return filterWalk(gen, p.filterBase, p.pendingQuery)
400. }
401. 
402. // ApplyFilterResult installs a completed filter walk, ignoring a result whose
403. // generation has been superseded by newer typing.
404. func (p *DirectoryPicker) ApplyFilterResult(msg FilterResultMsg) {
405. 	if msg.gen != p.filterGen {
406. 		return
407. 	}
408. 	p.pending = false
409. 	p.filterRoot = msg.root
410. 	p.matchCount = msg.count
411. 	p.flattenFrom(msg.root)
412. 	p.cursorToFirstMatch()
413. }
414. 
415. func filterTick(gen int) tea.Cmd {
416. 	return tea.Tick(filterDebounce, func(time.Time) tea.Msg {
417. 		return FilterTickMsg{Gen: gen}
418. 	})
419. }
420.

result.content:
1. package pane
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"fmt"
5. 	"strconv"
6. 	"strings"
7. 
8. 	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/key"
9. 	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/viewport"
10. 	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
11. 	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
12. 	"github.com/charmbracelet/x/ansi"
13. 
14. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/tui/component"
15. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/tui/theme"
16. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/worktree"
17. )
18. 
19. // DiffMsg carries the computed diff of a worktree session against its base
20. // commit. sessionID tags the session it was computed for, so a diff that
21. // arrives after the selection moved is dropped rather than shown under the
22. // wrong session. An empty text with no err is a clean worktree (no changes).
23. type DiffMsg struct {
24. 	sessionID string
25. 	text      string
26. 	added     int
27. 	removed   int
28. 	err       error
29. }
30. 
31. // SessionID is the session the diff was computed for. The root reads it to drop
32. // a delivery whose session is no longer selected.
33. func (m DiffMsg) SessionID() string {
34. 	return m.sessionID
35. }
36. 
37. // NewDiffErr builds a DiffMsg carrying err for sessionID. The root uses it to
38. // surface a workspace it could not resolve as the diff's error state.
39. func NewDiffErr(sessionID string, err error) DiffMsg {
40. 	return DiffMsg{sessionID: sessionID, err: err}
41. }
42. 
43. // DiffSession is the minimal set of facts the Diff body needs about the
44. // selected session to choose its render state without the pane reaching into
45. // the registry: whether one is selected, whether it is a worktree session, and
46. // its id so a not-yet-loaded selection shows the loading state.
47. type DiffSession struct {
48. 	Selected     bool
49. 	ID           string
50. 	Branch       string
51. 	WorktreePath string
52. 	BaseCommit   string
53. }
54. 
55. // Diff owns the Diff tab's scrollable viewport and the last computed diff: the
56. // session it was computed for, its text and add/remove counts, and any error.
57. // theme is the resolved theme the colorizer uses; the root refreshes it via
58. // SetTheme when the config editor changes it.
59. type Diff struct {
60. 	theme   theme.Theme
61. 	vp      viewport.Model
62. 	sid     string
63. 	text    string
64. 	added   int
65. 	removed int
66. 	err     error
67. }
68. 
69. // NewDiff builds a Diff with its viewport configured against theme.
70. func NewDiff(theme theme.Theme) Diff {
71. 	return Diff{theme: theme, vp: newDiffViewport()}
72. }
73. 
74. // SetTheme refreshes the theme the colorizer renders with. The root calls it
75. // when the in-cockpit config editor changes the theme.
76. func (d *Diff) SetTheme(theme theme.Theme) {
77. 	d.theme = theme
78. }
79. 
80. // newDiffViewport builds the Diff tab's scrollable viewport with a keymap that
81. // avoids the cockpit list bindings: it scrolls with PageUp/PageDown and the
82. // ctrl+f/ctrl+b/ctrl+u/ctrl+d chords and leaves the bare arrow keys to the list
83. // so up/down keep moving the session cursor (which re-targets the diff).
84. func newDiffViewport() viewport.Model {
85. 	vp := viewport.New(0, 0)
86. 	vp.KeyMap = viewport.KeyMap{
87. 		PageDown:     key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("pgdown", "ctrl+f")),
88. 		PageUp:       key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("pgup", "ctrl+b")),
89. 		HalfPageDown: key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+d")),
90. 		HalfPageUp:   key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+u")),
91. 	}
92. 	return vp
93. }
94. 
95. // Update forwards a message to the diff viewport so it scrolls. The root calls
96. // it on the Diff tab in list mode.
97. func (d *Diff) Update(msg tea.Msg) {
98. 	d.vp, _ = d.vp.Update(msg)
99. }
100. 
101. // EnsureCmd returns a command that computes the selected worktree session's
102. // diff against baseCommit, when that diff is not already loaded for sessionID.
103. // It is a no-op (nil) when the diff for the selection is already shown, so it
104. // fires only on a selection change or a switch to the Diff tab, never per tick.
105. // A plain (non-worktree) session — empty branch, worktree or base — loads an
106. // empty diff; Body renders the explanatory state for it. The root computes
107. // repoPath, home and workspaceID from the selected session and its workspace
108. // and passes them in, so the pane runs only the worktree diff itself.
109. func (d *Diff) EnsureCmd(
110. 	sessionID, repoPath, home, workspaceID, worktreePath, baseCommit string,
111. ) tea.Cmd {
112. 	if sessionID == "" || d.sid == sessionID {
113. 		return nil
114. 	}
115. 	return diffCmd(
116. 		sessionID,
117. 		repoPath,
118. 		home,
119. 		workspaceID,
120. 		worktreePath,
121. 		baseCommit,
122. 	)
123. }
124. 
125. // RefreshCmd recomputes the diff for the already-loaded selection, bypassing the
126. // EnsureCmd no-op guard so the live-refresh tick can pick up changes an agent
127. // makes in the worktree without the user re-selecting the row. It is for
128. // worktree sessions only: a plain session — empty base or worktree — returns nil
129. // rather than reloading the empty state every tick. Apply keeps the scroll
130. // position across a refresh, so the pane updates in place.
131. func (d *Diff) RefreshCmd(
132. 	sessionID, repoPath, home, workspaceID, worktreePath, baseCommit string,
133. ) tea.Cmd {
134. 	if sessionID == "" || baseCommit == "" || worktreePath == "" {
135. 		return nil
136. 	}
137. 	return diffCmd(
138. 		sessionID,
139. 		repoPath,
140. 		home,
141. 		workspaceID,
142. 		worktreePath,
143. 		baseCommit,
144. 	)
145. }
146. 
147. // diffCmd builds the command that computes a session's diff and tags the result
148. // with sessionID. A plain (non-worktree) session — empty base or worktree —
149. // yields an empty DiffMsg so Body renders its explanatory state; a worktree
150. // session runs the full diff against baseCommit. It is the shared body of
151. // EnsureCmd (selection change) and RefreshCmd (live tick).
152. func diffCmd(
153. 	sessionID, repoPath, home, workspaceID, worktreePath, baseCommit string,
154. ) tea.Cmd {
155. 	sid := sessionID
156. 	if baseCommit == "" || worktreePath == "" {
157. 		return func() tea.Msg { return DiffMsg{sessionID: sid} }
158. 	}
159. 	repo, wt, base, wsID := repoPath, worktreePath, baseCommit, workspaceID
160. 	return func() tea.Msg {
161. 		res, err := worktree.New(repo, home, wsID).Diff(wt, base)
162. 		if err != nil {
163. 			return DiffMsg{sessionID: sid, err: err}
164. 		}
165. 		return DiffMsg{
166. 			sessionID: sid, text: res.Text,
167. 			added: res.Added, removed: res.Removed,
168. 		}
169. 	}
170. }
171. 
172. // Apply stores a computed diff for rendering. The root has already dropped a
173. // delivery whose session is no longer selected. It loads the colorized content
174. // into the viewport, scrolling back to the top only when the diff is for a
175. // different session than the one shown — a live refresh of the same session
176. // keeps the scroll position so the pane updates in place rather than jumping to
177. // the top each tick. The root sizes the viewport (Size) before applying so the
178. // paging math matches the current pane.
179. func (d *Diff) Apply(msg DiffMsg) tea.Cmd {
180. 	changed := d.sid != msg.sessionID
181. 	d.sid = msg.sessionID
182. 	d.err = msg.err
183. 	d.text = msg.text
184. 	d.added, d.removed = msg.added, msg.removed
185. 	d.vp.SetContent(renderDiff(d.theme, msg.text, d.vp.Width))
186. 	if changed {
187. 		d.vp.SetYOffset(0)
188. 	}
189. 	return nil
190. }
191. 
192. // SID is the session id the loaded diff was computed for. The root reads it to
193. // drop a delivery whose session is no longer selected.
194. func (d Diff) SID() string {
195. 	return d.sid
196. }
197. 
198. // Size sizes the diff viewport to the pane body minus the summary line, so its
199. // paging math and render match what Body draws. The root runs it on resize and
200. // whenever a diff is loaded, never per tick.
201. func (d *Diff) Size(w, h int) {
202. 	d.vp.Width = max(w, 1)
203. 	d.vp.Height = max(h-1, 1)
204. }
205. 
206. // Body renders the Diff tab: a colorized git diff of the selected worktree
207. // session against its recorded base commit, in a scrollable viewport under an
208. // additions/deletions summary line. With no session selected it says so; a
209. // plain (non-worktree) session shows an explanatory state rather than an error;
210. // a worktree whose diff is not yet computed shows a loading state; and a clean
211. // worktree shows an empty state.
212. func (d Diff) Body(t theme.Theme, sess DiffSession, w, h int) string {
213. 	if !sess.Selected {
214. 		return t.DimStyle.Render("No session selected.")
215. 	}
216. 	if sess.Branch == "" || sess.WorktreePath == "" || sess.BaseCommit == "" {
217. 		return t.DimStyle.Render(
218. 			"Diff is only available for worktree sessions.",
219. 		)
220. 	}
221. 	if d.sid != sess.ID {
222. 		return t.DimStyle.Render("Loading diff…")
223. 	}
224. 	if d.err != nil {
225. 		return t.ErrorStyle.Render("diff error: " + d.err.Error())
226. 	}
227. 	if strings.TrimSpace(d.text) == "" {
228. 		return t.DimStyle.Render("No changes.")
229. 	}
230. 
231. 	vp := d.vp
232. 	vp.Width = max(w, 1)
233. 	vp.Height = max(h-1, 1)
234. 	return diffSummaryLine(t, d.added, d.removed) + "\n" + vp.View()
235. }
236. 
237. // diffSummaryLine renders the "N additions(+) / M deletions(-)" header above
238. // the diff, the additions in the add colour and the deletions in the delete
239. // colour.
240. func diffSummaryLine(theme theme.Theme, added, removed int) string {
241. 	return theme.DiffAddStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d additions(+)", added)) +
242. 		theme.DimStyle.Render(" / ") +
243. 		theme.DiffDelStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d deletions(-)", removed))
244. }
245. 
246. // gutterDigits is the width of each line-number column in the diff gutter, and
247. // gutterWidth is the full gutter the line-number columns and their separators
248. // occupy before a line's content: "<old> <new> ".
249. const (
250. 	gutterDigits = 4
251. 	gutterWidth  = gutterDigits*2 + 2
252. )
253. 
254. // tab is what a literal tab expands to in rendered diff content, so columns line
255. // up under a fixed-width gutter rather than jumping at the terminal's tab stops.
256. const tab = "    "
257. 
258. // renderDiff turns a plain unified git diff into the readable pane body, in the
259. // spirit of git-delta: each file gets a styled header bar (the diff/index/mode
260. // and ---/+++ noise lines are dropped), each hunk a dim rule carrying its
261. // section context, every line an old/new number gutter, and added and removed
262. // lines a full-width colour band so changes scan as blocks rather than as lone
263. // tinted characters. git emits the diff uncoloured, so the cockpit lays this out
264. // itself to match the theme. width is the viewport width the bands fill to.
265. func renderDiff(t theme.Theme, text string, width int) string {
266. 	width = max(width, 1)
267. 	contentW := max(width-gutterWidth, 1)
268. 
269. 	var (
270. 		b            strings.Builder
271. 		oldLn, newLn int
272. 		firstFile    = true
273. 	)
274. 	for _, line := range strings.Split(text, "\n") {
275. 		switch {
276. 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "diff --git"):
277. 			if !firstFile {
278. 				b.WriteByte('\n')
279. 			}
280. 			firstFile = false
281. 			b.WriteString(t.DiffFileStyle.Render(
282. 				component.Pad("▌ "+diffPath(line), width),
283. 			))
284. 			b.WriteByte('\n')
285. 		case isDiffNoise(line):
286. 			continue
287. 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "@@"):
288. 			oldLn, newLn = parseHunk(line)
289. 			b.WriteString(hunkRule(t, line, width))
290. 			b.WriteByte('\n')
291. 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "+"):
292. 			b.WriteString(diffLine(
293. 				t.DiffAddLineStyle, t.DiffAddStyle.GetForeground(),
294. 				t, "", newLn, "+", line[1:], contentW,
295. 			))
296. 			b.WriteByte('\n')
297. 			newLn++
298. 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "-"):
299. 			b.WriteString(diffLine(
300. 				t.DiffDelLineStyle, t.DiffDelStyle.GetForeground(),
301. 				t, itoa(oldLn), 0, "-", line[1:], contentW,
302. 			))
303. 			b.WriteByte('\n')
304. 			oldLn++
305. 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "\\"):
306. 			b.WriteString(t.DiffMetaStyle.Render("  " + line))
307. 			b.WriteByte('\n')
308. 		default:
309. 			content := line
310. 			if strings.HasPrefix(line, " ") {
311. 				content = line[1:]
312. 			}
313. 			b.WriteString(contextLine(t, oldLn, newLn, content, contentW))
314. 			b.WriteByte('\n')
315. 			oldLn++
316. 			newLn++
317. 		}
318. 	}
319. 	return strings.TrimRight(b.String(), "\n")
320. }
321. 
322. // diffLine renders one added or removed line: the gutter (only the relevant
323. // side numbered) followed by the sign and content on a full-width colour band,
324. // with the sign in the strong add/remove colour over the same band.
325. func diffLine(
326. 	band lipgloss.Style,
327. 	signColor lipgloss.TerminalColor,
328. 	t theme.Theme,
329. 	oldStr string, newLn int,
330. 	sign, content string,
331. 	contentW int,
332. ) string {
333. 	newStr := ""
334. 	if newLn > 0 {
335. 		newStr = itoa(newLn)
336. 	}
337. 	body := component.Pad(sign+expandTabs(content), contentW)
338. 	signed := band.Foreground(signColor).Bold(true).Render(sign) +
339. 		band.Render(strings.TrimPrefix(body, sign))
340. 	return gutter(t, oldStr, newStr) + signed
341. }
342. 
343. // contextLine renders an unchanged line: both line numbers in the dim gutter and
344. // the content aligned under the sign column in the default colour, with no band,
345. // so the surrounding code stays legible while the changed lines carry the tint.
346. func contextLine(
347. 	t theme.Theme,
348. 	oldLn, newLn int,
349. 	content string,
350. 	contentW int,
351. ) string {
352. 	return gutter(t, itoa(oldLn), itoa(newLn)) +
353. 		component.Pad(" "+expandTabs(content), contentW)
354. }
355. 
356. // gutter renders the old/new line-number columns, dimmed; an empty string leaves
357. // that side blank (an added line has no old number, a removed line no new one).
358. func gutter(t theme.Theme, oldStr, newStr string) string {
359. 	return t.DiffGutterStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf(
360. 		"%*s %*s ", gutterDigits, oldStr, gutterDigits, newStr,
361. 	))
362. }
363. 
364. // hunkRule renders a hunk as a dim horizontal rule carrying the section context
365. // git puts after the second "@@" (often the enclosing function), so hunks read
366. // as labelled separators rather than raw @@ coordinates.
367. func hunkRule(t theme.Theme, line string, width int) string {
368. 	head := "──"
369. 	if section := hunkSection(line); section != "" {
370. 		head = "── " + section + " "
371. 	}
372. 	if pad := width - ansi.StringWidth(head); pad > 0 {
373. 		head += strings.Repeat("─", pad)
374. 	}
375. 	return t.DiffMetaStyle.Render(head)
376. }
377. 
378. // isDiffNoise reports whether line is a unified-diff metadata line the pane hides
379. // because the file header bar already conveys what changed: the index/mode lines
380. // and the ---/+++ path lines.

result.content:
1. package modal
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"fmt"
5. 	"strings"
6. 
7. 	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
8. 	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9. 
10. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/launch"
11. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/tui/theme"
12. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/worktree"
13. )
14. 
15. // Create-form fields, in tab order: the directory comes first and the branch
16. // sits under it, since picking where a session runs is the common case and a
17. // branch (a worktree session) is the opt-in. The profile selector is the last
18. // field and is not a text input; the rest are.
19. const (
20. 	fieldDir = iota
21. 	fieldBranch
22. 	fieldTitle
23. 	fieldPrompt
24. 	fieldProgram
25. 	fieldAutonomous
26. 	fieldProfile
27. 	fieldCount
28. )
29. 
30. // FormSubmitMsg is emitted when the user accepts the create form; the parent
31. // builds the session.
32. type FormSubmitMsg struct{}
33. 
34. // FormCancelMsg is emitted when the user dismisses the create form.
35. type FormCancelMsg struct{}
36. 
37. // FormPickDirMsg is emitted when the user opens the directory browser over the
38. // form.
39. type FormPickDirMsg struct{}
40. 
41. // FormPickBranchMsg is emitted when the user opens the branch picker over the
42. // form.
43. type FormPickBranchMsg struct{}
44. 
45. func formSubmit() tea.Msg { return FormSubmitMsg{} }
46. 
47. func formCancel() tea.Msg { return FormCancelMsg{} }
48. 
49. func formPickDir() tea.Msg { return FormPickDirMsg{} }
50. 
51. func formPickBranch() tea.Msg { return FormPickBranchMsg{} }
52. 
53. // CreateForm collects the inputs for a new session. The two session shapes share
54. // one form: leaving Branch empty creates a plain session that runs in a working
55. // directory; entering a branch opts into a worktree session created on it.
56. //
57. // The form has two modes, set at construction. Inside a workspace WorkspaceRepo
58. // holds that workspace's repository root: the Directory field is dropped (a
59. // session created in a workspace is anchored to its repo, never a free-form path),
60. // the working directory of a plain session is the repo root, and a worktree
61. // session's branch resolves against that same repo. Outside any workspace
62. // WorkspaceRepo is empty: the Directory field returns, and the path picked there
63. // is the session's only anchor — it both sets a plain session's working directory
64. // and resolves the repository a worktree session's branch operates on.
65. //
66. // The Branch field is only meaningful when a repository backs it, so it is enabled
67. // only when BranchRepo is set, and disabled (skipped in tab order and shown
68. // dimmed) otherwise. BranchRepo is that repository toplevel: the fixed
69. // WorkspaceRepo in workspace mode, or the toplevel re-derived from the Directory
70. // field in orphan mode (empty until a directory inside a git repository is
71. // chosen). Title and program are optional, and a profile is chosen from the
72. // workspace's profiles with the default (first) preselected. The program field
73. // shows every agent detected on PATH plus a bare-shell entry as a visible menu;
74. // ←/→ move the selection and typing overrides it with any program name outside the
75. // known set.
76. type CreateForm struct {
77. 	theme         theme.Theme
78. 	inputs        []textinput.Model
79. 	BranchRepo    string
80. 	WorkspaceRepo string
81. 	profiles      []string
82. 	profIdx       int
83. 	programs      []string
84. 	shell         string
85. 	progIdx       int
86. 	autonomous    bool
87. 	focus         int
88. 	err           string
89. }
90. 
91. // NewCreateForm builds the create form for a workspace's profiles, styled with
92. // theme. workspaceRepo is the active workspace's repository root, or "" when wasa
93. // is creating a session outside any workspace (the orphan/scratch case). A
94. // non-empty workspaceRepo drops the Directory field and anchors the session to
95. // that repo; an empty one restores the Directory field, which starts focused and
96. // empty, as the session's only anchor.
97. func NewCreateForm(
98. 	theme theme.Theme,
99. 	profiles []string,
100. 	workspaceRepo string,
101. ) CreateForm {
102. 	dir := textinput.New()
103. 	dir.Placeholder = "ctrl+f to browse, or empty for here"
104. 	dir.CharLimit = 4096
105. 
106. 	branch := textinput.New()
107. 	branch.Placeholder = "ctrl+f to pick a branch (worktree session)"
108. 	branch.CharLimit = 200
109. 
110. 	title := textinput.New()
111. 	title.Placeholder = "optional title"
112. 	title.CharLimit = 200
113. 
114. 	prompt := textinput.New()
115. 	prompt.Placeholder = "optional — what this session should do"
116. 	prompt.CharLimit = 4096
117. 
118. 	shell := launch.Shell()
119. 	programs := append(launch.DetectAgents(), shell)
120. 
121. 	program := textinput.New()
122. 	program.CharLimit = 200
123. 	program.SetValue(programs[0])
124. 
125. 	f := CreateForm{
126. 		theme:         theme,
127. 		inputs:        []textinput.Model{dir, branch, title, prompt, program},
128. 		profiles:      profiles,
129. 		programs:      programs,
130. 		shell:         shell,
131. 		WorkspaceRepo: workspaceRepo,
132. 	}
133. 	f.SyncBranchRepo()
134. 	f.setFocus(f.firstField())
135. 	return f
136. }
137. 
138. // dirEnabled reports whether the Directory field is present: only in orphan mode
139. // (no active workspace), where the picked path is the session's anchor. Inside a
140. // workspace the field is dropped, since the session is anchored to the workspace's
141. // repository.
142. func (f CreateForm) dirEnabled() bool {
143. 	return f.WorkspaceRepo == ""
144. }
145. 
146. // firstField is the field focused when the form opens: the Directory field in
147. // orphan mode, or the Branch field when the Directory field has been dropped
148. // inside a workspace.
149. func (f CreateForm) firstField() int {
150. 	if f.dirEnabled() {
151. 		return fieldDir
152. 	}
153. 	return fieldBranch
154. }
155. 
156. // SyncBranchRepo re-derives the repository the Branch field operates on. In
157. // workspace mode it is fixed to the workspace's repository, so a worktree session
158. // always lands in the workspace's repo. In orphan mode it follows the Directory
159. // field's current value, so the field's enabled state and the branch picker
160. // reflect the directory as currently chosen: an empty Directory has no branch
161. // context and disables the field; a Directory inside a git repository resolves to
162. // that repository; anything else (a plain directory, a path that does not exist,
163. // git absent) leaves it empty, disabling the field.
164. func (f *CreateForm) SyncBranchRepo() {
165. 	if f.WorkspaceRepo != "" {
166. 		f.BranchRepo = f.WorkspaceRepo
167. 		return
168. 	}
169. 	f.BranchRepo = branchRepoFor(f.Dir())
170. }
171. 
172. // branchRepoFor resolves the repository toplevel that should back the Branch
173. // field for dir. It returns an empty string when dir is empty or not inside a git
174. // repository, mirroring repoBranches in swallowing resolution failures rather than
175. // surfacing them.
176. func branchRepoFor(dir string) string {
177. 	if dir == "" {
178. 		return ""
179. 	}
180. 	top, err := worktree.Toplevel(dir)
181. 	if err != nil {
182. 		return ""
183. 	}
184. 	return top
185. }
186. 
187. // Update routes a message into the form, returning the updated form and a
188. // command that emits a FormSubmitMsg, FormCancelMsg, FormPickDirMsg or
189. // FormPickBranchMsg on the key that triggers it, or nil otherwise.
190. func (f CreateForm) Update(msg tea.Msg) (CreateForm, tea.Cmd) {
191. 	if key, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
192. 		switch key.String() {
193. 		case "esc":
194. 			return f, formCancel
195. 		case "ctrl+f":
196. 			switch f.focus {
197. 			case fieldDir:
198. 				return f, formPickDir
199. 			case fieldBranch:
200. 				if f.BranchEnabled() {
201. 					return f, formPickBranch
202. 				}
203. 			}
204. 			return f, nil
205. 		case "enter":
206. 			return f, formSubmit
207. 		case "tab", "down":
208. 			f.focusNext()
209. 			return f, nil
210. 		case "shift+tab", "up":
211. 			f.focusPrev()
212. 			return f, nil
213. 		case "left", "right":
214. 			switch f.focus {
215. 			case fieldProfile:
216. 				f.cycleProfile(key.String() == "right")
217. 				return f, nil
218. 			case fieldProgram:
219. 				f.cycleProgram(key.String() == "right")
220. 				return f, nil
221. 			case fieldAutonomous:
222. 				f.toggleAutonomous()
223. 				return f, nil
224. 			}
225. 		case " ":
226. 			if f.focus == fieldAutonomous {
227. 				f.toggleAutonomous()
228. 				return f, nil
229. 			}
230. 		}
231. 	}
232. 
233. 	if f.focus < len(f.inputs) {
234. 		var cmd tea.Cmd
235. 		f.inputs[f.focus], cmd = f.inputs[f.focus].Update(msg)
236. 		if f.focus == fieldDir {
237. 			f.SyncBranchRepo()
238. 		}
239. 		return f, cmd
240. 	}
241. 	return f, nil
242. }
243. 
244. // SetProfiles replaces the profile menu with names, the profiles of the
245. // workspace the form's current Directory resolves to. It preserves the user's
246. // selection by name when that profile still exists, and otherwise falls back to
247. // the default (first) profile, so switching to a directory in a different
248. // repository never leaves a profile selected that is invalid there.
249. func (f *CreateForm) SetProfiles(names []string) {
250. 	cur := ""
251. 	if f.profIdx < len(f.profiles) {
252. 		cur = f.profiles[f.profIdx]
253. 	}
254. 	f.profiles = names
255. 	f.profIdx = 0
256. 	for i, n := range names {
257. 		if n == cur {
258. 			f.profIdx = i
259. 			break
260. 		}
261. 	}
262. }
263. 
264. func (f *CreateForm) cycleProfile(forward bool) {
265. 	if len(f.profiles) == 0 {
266. 		return
267. 	}
268. 	if forward {
269. 		f.profIdx = (f.profIdx + 1) % len(f.profiles)
270. 		return
271. 	}
272. 	f.profIdx = (f.profIdx - 1 + len(f.profiles)) % len(f.profiles)
273. }
274. 
275. // cycleProgram steps the program field through the detected-agents-plus-shell
276. // menu, writing the chosen program into the text input. Typing afterwards
277. // overrides the selection, so a program outside the known set stays reachable.
278. func (f *CreateForm) cycleProgram(forward bool) {
279. 	if len(f.programs) == 0 {
280. 		return
281. 	}
282. 	if forward {
283. 		f.progIdx = (f.progIdx + 1) % len(f.programs)
284. 	} else {
285. 		f.progIdx = (f.progIdx - 1 + len(f.programs)) % len(f.programs)
286. 	}
287. 	f.inputs[fieldProgram].SetValue(f.programs[f.progIdx])
288. }
289. 
290. // toggleAutonomous flips the autonomous (skip-permissions) toggle, but only when
291. // the selected program has a known autonomous flag — there is nothing to enable
292. // for a shell or an unknown program.
293. func (f *CreateForm) toggleAutonomous() {
294. 	if !f.autonomousEnabled() {
295. 		return
296. 	}
297. 	f.autonomous = !f.autonomous
298. }
299. 
300. // autonomousEnabled reports whether the autonomous toggle is usable: only when
301. // the program as currently typed maps to a known skip-permissions flag. It reads
302. // the live Program field so a free-typed agent name is honoured too.
303. func (f CreateForm) autonomousEnabled() bool {
304. 	return launch.AutonomousAvailable(
305. 		strings.TrimSpace(f.inputs[fieldProgram].Value()),
306. 	)
307. }
308. 
309. // Dir is the Directory field's current value, trimmed. It seeds the directory
310. // browser's starting point when the picker is opened.
311. func (f CreateForm) Dir() string {
312. 	return strings.TrimSpace(f.inputs[fieldDir].Value())
313. }
314. 
315. // SetDir writes a path chosen in the directory picker into the Directory field
316. // and moves focus to it, so the picked value is visible and editable on return.
317. func (f *CreateForm) SetDir(path string) {
318. 	f.inputs[fieldDir].SetValue(path)
319. 	f.SyncBranchRepo()
320. 	f.setFocus(fieldDir)
321. }
322. 
323. // SetBranch writes a branch chosen or typed in the branch picker into the Branch
324. // field and moves focus to it.
325. func (f *CreateForm) SetBranch(branch string) {
326. 	f.inputs[fieldBranch].SetValue(branch)
327. 	f.setFocus(fieldBranch)
328. }
329. 
330. // BranchEnabled reports whether the Branch field is usable: only when the chosen
331. // Directory resolves to a git repository (or, with an empty Directory, when wasa
332. // was launched inside one), since a worktree session is created against that
333. // repository. BranchRepo is kept in sync with the Directory field, so this reads
334. // the cached resolution rather than shelling out to git on every render.
335. func (f CreateForm) BranchEnabled() bool {
336. 	return f.BranchRepo != ""
337. }
338. 
339. func (f *CreateForm) focusNext() { f.setFocus(f.stepFocus(1)) }
340. 
341. func (f *CreateForm) focusPrev() { f.setFocus(f.stepFocus(-1)) }
342. 
343. // stepFocus returns the next focusable field in the given direction, skipping
344. // the Directory field when it has been dropped inside a workspace and the Branch
345. // and Autonomous fields when they are disabled, so tab never lands on a dead or
346. // absent input.
347. func (f CreateForm) stepFocus(dir int) int {
348. 	i := f.focus
349. 	for range fieldCount {
350. 		i = (i + dir + fieldCount) % fieldCount
351. 		if i == fieldDir && !f.dirEnabled() {
352. 			continue
353. 		}
354. 		if i == fieldBranch && !f.BranchEnabled() {
355. 			continue
356. 		}
357. 		if i == fieldAutonomous && !f.autonomousEnabled() {
358. 			continue
359. 		}
360. 		return i
361. 	}
362. 	return f.focus
363. }
364. 
365. func (f *CreateForm) setFocus(i int) {
366. 	f.focus = i
367. 	for j := range f.inputs {
368. 		if j == i {
369. 			f.inputs[j].Focus()
370. 		} else {
371. 			f.inputs[j].Blur()
372. 		}
373. 	}
374. }
375. 
376. // Params reads the form into launch.Params. A non-empty branch selects a
377. // worktree session and the working directory is ignored. An empty branch selects
378. // a plain session, run in the workspace's repository root inside a workspace, or
379. // in the Directory field's value in orphan mode.
380. func (f CreateForm) Params() launch.Params {
381. 	prof := ""
382. 	if f.profIdx < len(f.profiles) {
383. 		prof = f.profiles[f.profIdx]
384. 	}
385. 	program := strings.TrimSpace(f.inputs[fieldProgram].Value())
386. 	if f.autonomous && f.autonomousEnabled() {
387. 		program = launch.WithAutonomous(program)
388. 	}
389. 	p := launch.Params{
390. 		Title:         strings.TrimSpace(f.inputs[fieldTitle].Value()),
391. 		Program:       program,
392. 		Profile:       prof,
393. 		InitialPrompt: strings.TrimSpace(f.inputs[fieldPrompt].Value()),
394. 	}
395. 	branch := ""
396. 	if f.BranchEnabled() {
397. 		branch = strings.TrimSpace(f.inputs[fieldBranch].Value())
398. 	}
399. 	switch {
400. 	case branch != "":
401. 		p.Branch = branch
402. 	case f.WorkspaceRepo != "":
403. 		p.WorkingDir = f.WorkspaceRepo
404. 	default:
405. 		p.WorkingDir = strings.TrimSpace(f.inputs[fieldDir].Value())
406. 	}
407. 	return p
408. }
409. 
410. // View renders the create form.
411. func (f CreateForm) View() string {
412. 	var b strings.Builder
413. 	b.WriteString(f.theme.TitleStyle.Render("New session"))
414. 	b.WriteString("\n\n")
415. 
416. 	labels := []string{"Directory", "Branch", "Title", "Prompt"}
417. 	for i := fieldDir; i <= fieldPrompt; i++ {
418. 		if i == fieldDir && !f.dirEnabled() {
419. 			continue
420. 		}
421. 		b.WriteString(f.label(labels[i], i))
422. 		b.WriteString("\n")
423. 		if i == fieldBranch && !f.BranchEnabled() {
424. 			b.WriteString(
425. 				f.theme.DimStyle.Render("  (only inside a git repository)"),
426. 			)
427. 		} else {
428. 			b.WriteString(f.inputs[i].View())
429. 		}
430. 		b.WriteString("\n\n")
431. 	}
432. 
433. 	b.WriteString(f.label("Program", fieldProgram))
434. 	b.WriteString("\n")
435. 	b.WriteString(f.programView())
436. 	b.WriteString("\n\n")
437. 
438. 	b.WriteString(f.label("Autonomous", fieldAutonomous))
439. 	b.WriteString("\n")
440. 	b.WriteString(f.autonomousView())
441. 	b.WriteString("\n\n")
442. 
443. 	b.WriteString(f.label("Profile", fieldProfile))
444. 	b.WriteString("\n")
445. 	b.WriteString(f.profileView())
446. 	b.WriteString("\n\n")
447. 
448. 	if f.err != "" {
449. 		b.WriteString(f.theme.ErrorStyle.Render(f.err))
450. 		b.WriteString("\n\n")
451. 	}
452. 	browse := "ctrl+f pick branch"
453. 	if f.dirEnabled() {
454. 		browse = "ctrl+f browse dir/branch"
455. 	}
456. 	b.WriteString(f.theme.DimStyle.Render(
457. 		"tab/↑↓ move · ←/→/space choose/toggle · " +
458. 			browse + " · enter create · esc cancel",
459. 	))
460. 	return b.String()
461. }
462. 
463. func (f CreateForm) label(text string, field int) string {
464. 	if f.focus == field {
465. 		return f.theme.FocusedLabelStyle.Render("> " + text)
466. 	}
467. 	return f.theme.LabelStyle.Render("  " + text)
468. }
469. 
470. // programView renders the detected-agents-plus-shell menu inline, highlighting
471. // the active entry. A value typed outside the known set is shown as a trailing
472. // highlighted entry so free-text overrides stay visible.
473. func (f CreateForm) programView() string {
474. 	cur := strings.TrimSpace(f.inputs[fieldProgram].Value())
475. 	parts := make([]string, 0, len(f.programs)+1)
476. 	matched := false
477. 	for _, p := range f.programs {
478. 		if p == cur {
479. 			matched = true
480. 			parts = append(
481. 				parts,
482. 				f.theme.FocusedLabelStyle.Render("["+f.programLabel(p)+"]"),
483. 			)
484. 			continue
485. 		}
486. 		parts = append(parts, f.theme.DimStyle.Render(f.programLabel(p)))
487. 	}
488. 	if !matched && cur != "" {
489. 		parts = append(parts, f.theme.FocusedLabelStyle.Render("["+cur+"]"))
490. 	}
491. 	return "  " + strings.Join(parts, "   ")
492. }
493. 
494. // programLabel is the menu label for a program value: the shell entry shows as
495. // "shell" rather than its resolved path, every agent shows by name.
496. func (f CreateForm) programLabel(p string) string {
497. 	if p == f.shell {
498. 		return "shell"
499. 	}
500. 	return p
501. }
502. 
503. // autonomousView renders the skip-permissions toggle: a checkbox with an inline
504. // hint about the consequence when the selected program supports it, or a dim
505. // note that it is unavailable for a shell or unknown program.
506. func (f CreateForm) autonomousView() string {
507. 	if !f.autonomousEnabled() {
508. 		return f.theme.DimStyle.Render("  (not available for this program)")
509. 	}
510. 	box := "[ ]"
511. 	if f.autonomous {
512. 		box = "[x]"
513. 	}
514. 	hint := f.theme.DimStyle.Render(" runs without approval prompts")
515. 	return "  " + box + " skip permissions" + hint
516. }
517. 
518. func (f CreateForm) profileView() string {
519. 	if len(f.profiles) == 0 {
520. 		return f.theme.DimStyle.Render("  (no profiles)")

result.content:
1. package modal
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"strings"
5. 
6. 	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
7. 	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
8. 
9. 	"github.com/joakimcarlsson/wasa-cli/internal/tui/theme"
10. )
11. 
12. // ConfirmAcceptedMsg is emitted by a ConfirmDialog when the user accepts the
13. // prompt; the parent runs its stored confirm command in response.
14. type ConfirmAcceptedMsg struct{}
15. 
16. // ConfirmCancelledMsg is emitted by a ConfirmDialog when the user declines or
17. // dismisses the prompt; the parent returns to the list with no change.
18. type ConfirmCancelledMsg struct{}
19. 
20. // ConfirmDialog is a reusable yes/no modal: a titled, bordered box with a body
21. // prompt and two focusable buttons rendered like a web dialog. Left/right (or
22. // tab) move focus between the buttons, enter chooses the focused one, and the
23. // y/n/esc shortcuts choose directly. It owns only its presentation and focus
24. // state; the parent decides what confirming means. The cancel button starts
25. // focused so a stray enter on a destructive prompt cancels rather than confirms.
26. type ConfirmDialog struct {
27. 	theme       theme.Theme
28. 	title       string
29. 	body        string
30. 	confirmText string
31. 	cancelText  string
32. 	onConfirm   bool
33. 	danger      bool
34. }
35. 
36. // NewConfirmDialog builds a yes/no dialog with the given title, prebuilt body,
37. // button labels and destructive styling, styled with theme. The cancel button
38. // starts focused.
39. func NewConfirmDialog(
40. 	theme theme.Theme,
41. 	title, body, confirmText, cancelText string,
42. 	danger bool,
43. ) ConfirmDialog {
44. 	return ConfirmDialog{
45. 		theme:       theme,
46. 		title:       title,
47. 		body:        body,
48. 		confirmText: confirmText,
49. 		cancelText:  cancelText,
50. 		danger:      danger,
51. 	}
52. }
53. 
54. // Update routes a key message into the dialog, returning the updated dialog and
55. // a command that emits ConfirmAcceptedMsg or ConfirmCancelledMsg on the key that
56. // settles the choice, or nil otherwise.
57. func (d ConfirmDialog) Update(msg tea.Msg) (ConfirmDialog, tea.Cmd) {
58. 	key, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg)
59. 	if !ok {
60. 		return d, nil
61. 	}
62. 	switch key.String() {
63. 	case "left", "right", "tab", "shift+tab", "h", "l":
64. 		d.onConfirm = !d.onConfirm
65. 		return d, nil
66. 	case "enter":
67. 		if d.onConfirm {
68. 			return d, accepted
69. 		}
70. 		return d, cancelled
71. 	case "y":
72. 		return d, accepted
73. 	case "n", "esc", "q":
74. 		return d, cancelled
75. 	}
76. 	return d, nil
77. }
78. 
79. func accepted() tea.Msg { return ConfirmAcceptedMsg{} }
80. 
81. func cancelled() tea.Msg { return ConfirmCancelledMsg{} }
82. 
83. // View renders the dialog box.
84. func (d ConfirmDialog) View() string {
85. 	heading := d.theme.TitleStyle
86. 	if d.danger {
87. 		heading = d.theme.ErrorStyle.Bold(true)
88. 	}
89. 	top := heading.Render(d.title) + "\n\n" + d.body
90. 
91. 	buttons := d.buttons()
92. 	if w := lipgloss.Width(top); lipgloss.Width(buttons) < w {
93. 		buttons = lipgloss.PlaceHorizontal(w, lipgloss.Center, buttons)
94. 	}
95. 	return d.theme.ModalStyle.Render(top + "\n\n" + buttons)
96. }
97. 
98. // buttons renders the cancel/confirm pair, filling the focused one and dimming
99. // the other. The confirm button fills red when the dialog is destructive.
100. func (d ConfirmDialog) buttons() string {
101. 	cancelStyle, confirmStyle := d.theme.BtnInactiveStyle, d.theme.BtnInactiveStyle
102. 	if d.onConfirm {
103. 		confirmStyle = d.theme.BtnConfirmStyle
104. 		if d.danger {
105. 			confirmStyle = d.theme.BtnDangerStyle
106. 		}
107. 	} else {
108. 		cancelStyle = d.theme.BtnCancelStyle
109. 	}
110. 	return strings.Join([]string{
111. 		cancelStyle.Render(d.cancelText),
112. 		confirmStyle.Render(d.confirmText),
113. 	}, "  ")
114. }
115.

52. // Model contains the program's state as well as its core functions.

59. 	// and, in response, update the model and/or send a command.

63. 	// view is rendered after every Update.

67. // NewView is a helper function to create a new [View] with the given styled
68. // string. A styled string represents text with styles and hyperlinks encoded
69. // as ANSI escape codes.
70. //
71. // Example:
72. //
73. //	```go
74. //	v := tea.NewView("Hello, World!")
75. //	```
76. func NewView(s string) View {
77. 	var view View
78. 	view.SetContent(s)
79. 	return view
80. }

190. // KeyPressMsg represents a key press message.
191. type KeyPressMsg Key
192. 
193. // String implements [fmt.Stringer] and is quite useful for matching key
194. // events. For details, on what this returns see [Key.String].
195. func (k KeyPressMsg) String() string {
196. 	return Key(k).String()
197. }
198. 
199. // Keystroke returns the keystroke representation of the [Key]. While less type
200. // safe than looking at the individual fields, it will usually be more
201. // convenient and readable to use this method when matching against keys.
202. //
203. // Note that modifier keys are always printed in the following order:
204. //   - ctrl
205. //   - alt
206. //   - shift
207. //   - meta
208. //   - hyper
209. //   - super
210. //
211. // For example, you'll always see "ctrl+shift+alt+a" and never
212. // "shift+ctrl+alt+a".
213. func (k KeyPressMsg) Keystroke() string {
214. 	return uv.Key(k).Keystroke()
215. }
216. 
217. // Key returns the underlying key event. This is a syntactic sugar for casting
218. // the key event to a [Key].
219. func (k KeyPressMsg) Key() Key {
220. 	return Key(k)
221. }
222. 
223. // KeyReleaseMsg represents a key release message.
224. type KeyReleaseMsg Key
225. 
226. // String implements [fmt.Stringer] and is quite useful for matching key
227. // events. For details, on what this returns see [Key.String].

231. 
232. // Keystroke returns the keystroke representation of the [Key]. While less type

235. //
236. // Note that modifier keys are always printed in the following order:

241. //   - hyper
242. //   - super
243. //
244. // For example, you'll always see "ctrl+shift+alt+a" and never
245. // "shift+ctrl+alt+a".
246. func (k KeyReleaseMsg) Keystroke() string {
247. 	return uv.Key(k).Keystroke()
248. }
249. 
250. // Key returns the underlying key event. This is a convenience method and
251. // syntactic sugar to satisfy the [KeyMsg] interface, and cast the key event to
252. // [Key].
253. func (k KeyReleaseMsg) Key() Key {
254. 	return Key(k)
255. }

41. // New returns a new model with the given width and height as well as default
42. // key mappings.
43. func New(opts ...Option) (m Model) {
44. 	for _, opt := range opts {
45. 		opt(&m)
46. 	}
47. 	m.setInitialValues()
48. 	return m
49. }
50. 
51. // Model is the Bubble Tea model for this viewport element.
52. type Model struct {
53. 	width  int
54. 	height int
55. 	KeyMap KeyMap
56. 
57. 	// Whether or not to wrap text. If false, it'll allow horizontal scrolling
58. 	// instead.
59. 	SoftWrap bool
60. 
61. 	// Whether or not to fill to the height of the viewport with empty lines.
62. 	FillHeight bool
63. 
64. 	// Whether or not to respond to the mouse. The mouse must be enabled in
65. 	// Bubble Tea for this to work. For details, see the Bubble Tea docs.
66. 	MouseWheelEnabled bool
67. 
68. 	// The number of lines the mouse wheel will scroll. By default, this is 3.
69. 	MouseWheelDelta int
70. 
71. 	// yOffset is the vertical scroll position.
72. 	yOffset int
73. 
74. 	// xOffset is the horizontal scroll position.
75. 	xOffset int
76. 
77. 	// horizontalStep is the number of columns we move left or right during a
78. 	// default horizontal scroll.
79. 	horizontalStep int
80. 
81. 	// YPosition is the position of the viewport in relation to the terminal
82. 	// window. It's used in high performance rendering only.
83. 	YPosition int
84. 
85. 	// Style applies a lipgloss style to the viewport. Realistically, it's most
86. 	// useful for setting borders, margins and padding.
87. 	Style lipgloss.Style
88. 
89. 	// LeftGutterFunc allows to define a [GutterFunc] that adds a column into
90. 	// the left of the viewport, which is kept when horizontal scrolling.
91. 	// This can be used for things like line numbers, selection indicators,
92. 	// show statuses, etc. It is expected that the real-width (as measured by
93. 	// [lipgloss.Width]) of the returned value is always consistent, regardless
94. 	// of index, soft wrapping, etc.
95. 	LeftGutterFunc GutterFunc
96. 
97. 	initialized      bool
98. 	lines            []string
99. 	longestLineWidth int
100. 
101. 	// HighlightStyle highlights the ranges set with [SetHighligths].
102. 	HighlightStyle lipgloss.Style
103. 
104. 	// SelectedHighlightStyle highlights the highlight range focused during
105. 	// navigation.
106. 	// Use [SetHighligths] to set the highlight ranges, and [HightlightNext]
107. 	// and [HihglightPrevious] to navigate.
108. 	SelectedHighlightStyle lipgloss.Style
109. 
110. 	// StyleLineFunc allows to return a [lipgloss.Style] for each line.
111. 	// The argument is the line index.
112. 	StyleLineFunc func(int) lipgloss.Style
113. 
114. 	highlights []highlightInfo
115. 	hiIdx      int
116. }
117. 
118. // GutterFunc can be implemented and set into [Model.LeftGutterFunc].
119. //
120. // Example implementation showing line numbers:
121. //
122. //	func(info GutterContext) string {
123. //		if info.Soft {
124. //			return "     │ "
125. //		}
126. //		if info.Index >= info.TotalLines {
127. //			return "   ~ │ "
128. //		}
129. //		return fmt.Sprintf("%4d │ ", info.Index+1)
130. //	}
131. type GutterFunc func(GutterContext) string
132. 
133. // NoGutter is the default gutter used.
134. var NoGutter = func(GutterContext) string { return "" }
135. 
136. // GutterContext provides context to a [GutterFunc].
137. type GutterContext struct {
138. 	// Index is the line index of the line which the gutter is being rendered for.
139. 	Index int
140. 
141. 	// TotalLines is the total number of lines in the viewport.
142. 	TotalLines int
143. 
144. 	// Soft is whether or not the line is soft wrapped.
145. 	Soft bool
146. }
147. 
148. func (m *Model) setInitialValues() {
149. 	m.KeyMap = DefaultKeyMap()
150. 	m.MouseWheelEnabled = true
151. 	m.MouseWheelDelta = 3
152. 	m.horizontalStep = defaultHorizontalStep
153. 	m.LeftGutterFunc = NoGutter
154. 	m.initialized = true
155. }
156. 
157. // Init exists to satisfy the tea.Model interface for composability purposes.
158. func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
159. 	return nil
160. }
161. 
162. // Height returns the height of the viewport.
163. func (m Model) Height() int {
164. 	return m.height
165. }
166. 
167. // SetHeight sets the height of the viewport.
168. func (m *Model) SetHeight(h int) {
169. 	m.height = h
170. }
171. 
172. // Width returns the width of the viewport.
173. func (m Model) Width() int {
174. 	return m.width
175. }
176. 
177. // SetWidth sets the width of the viewport.
178. func (m *Model) SetWidth(w int) {
179. 	m.width = w
180. }
181. 
182. // AtTop returns whether or not the viewport is at the very top position.
183. func (m Model) AtTop() bool {
184. 	return m.YOffset() <= 0
185. }
186. 
187. // AtBottom returns whether or not the viewport is at or past the very bottom
188. // position.
189. func (m Model) AtBottom() bool {
190. 	return m.YOffset() >= m.maxYOffset()
191. }
192. 
193. // PastBottom returns whether or not the viewport is scrolled beyond the last
194. // line. This can happen when adjusting the viewport height.
195. func (m Model) PastBottom() bool {
196. 	return m.YOffset() > m.maxYOffset()
197. }
198. 
199. // ScrollPercent returns the amount scrolled as a float between 0 and 1.
200. func (m Model) ScrollPercent() float64 {
201. 	total, _, _ := m.calculateLine(0)
202. 	if m.Height() >= total {
203. 		return 1.0
204. 	}
205. 	y := float64(m.YOffset())
206. 	h := float64(m.Height())
207. 	t := float64(total)
208. 	v := y / (t - h)
209. 	return clamp(v, 0, 1)
210. }
211. 
212. // HorizontalScrollPercent returns the amount horizontally scrolled as a float
213. // between 0 and 1.
214. func (m Model) HorizontalScrollPercent() float64 {
215. 	if m.xOffset >= m.longestLineWidth-m.Width() {
216. 		return 1.0
217. 	}
218. 	y := float64(m.xOffset)
219. 	h := float64(m.Width())
220. 	t := float64(m.longestLineWidth)

266. // Key represents a Key press or release event. It contains information about
267. // the Key pressed, like the runes, the type of Key, and the modifiers pressed.
268. // There are a couple general patterns you could use to check for key presses
269. // or releases:
270. //
271. //	// Switch on the string representation of the key (shorter)
272. //	switch msg := msg.(type) {
273. //	case KeyPressMsg:
274. //	    switch msg.String() {
275. //	    case "enter":
276. //	        fmt.Println("you pressed enter!")
277. //	    case "a":
278. //	        fmt.Println("you pressed a!")
279. //	    }
280. //	}
281. //
282. //	// Switch on the key type (more foolproof)
283. //	switch msg := msg.(type) {
284. //	case KeyMsg:
285. //	    // catch both KeyPressMsg and KeyReleaseMsg
286. //	    switch key := msg.Key(); key.Code {
287. //	    case KeyEnter:
288. //	        fmt.Println("you pressed enter!")
289. //	    default:
290. //	        switch key.Text {
291. //	        case "a":
292. //	            fmt.Println("you pressed a!")
293. //	        }
294. //	    }
295. //	}
296. //
297. // Note that [Key.Text] will be empty for special keys like [KeyEnter],
298. // [KeyTab], and for keys that don't represent printable characters like key
299. // combos with modifier keys. In other words, [Key.Text] is populated only for
300. // keys that represent printable characters shifted or unshifted (like 'a',
301. // 'A', '1', '!', etc.).
302. type Key struct {
303. 	// Text contains the actual characters received. This usually the same as
304. 	// [Key.Code]. When [Key.Text] is non-empty, it indicates that the key
305. 	// pressed represents printable character(s).
306. 	Text string
307. 
308. 	// Mod represents modifier keys, like [ModCtrl], [ModAlt], and so on.
309. 	Mod KeyMod
310. 
311. 	// Code represents the key pressed. This is usually a special key like
312. 	// [KeyTab], [KeyEnter], [KeyF1], or a printable character like 'a'.
313. 	Code rune
314. 
315. 	// ShiftedCode is the actual, shifted key pressed by the user. For example,
316. 	// if the user presses shift+a, or caps lock is on, [Key.ShiftedCode] will
317. 	// be 'A' and [Key.Code] will be 'a'.
318. 	//
319. 	// In the case of non-latin keyboards, like Arabic, [Key.ShiftedCode] is the
320. 	// unshifted key on the keyboard.
321. 	//
322. 	// This is only available with the Kitty Keyboard Protocol or the Windows
323. 	// Console API.
324. 	ShiftedCode rune
325. 
326. 	// BaseCode is the key pressed according to the standard PC-101 key layout.
327. 	// On international keyboards, this is the key that would be pressed if the
328. 	// keyboard was set to US PC-101 layout.
329. 	//
330. 	// For example, if the user presses 'q' on a French AZERTY keyboard,
331. 	// [Key.BaseCode] will be 'q'.
332. 	//
333. 	// This is only available with the Kitty Keyboard Protocol or the Windows
334. 	// Console API.
335. 	BaseCode rune
336. 
337. 	// IsRepeat indicates whether the key is being held down and sending events
338. 	// repeatedly.
339. 	//
340. 	// This is only available with the Kitty Keyboard Protocol or the Windows
341. 	// Console API.
342. 	IsRepeat bool
343. }
344. 
345. // String implements [fmt.Stringer] and is quite useful for matching key
346. // events. It will return the textual representation of the [Key] if there is
347. // one, otherwise, it will fallback to [Key.Keystroke].
348. //
349. // For example, you'll always get "?" and instead of "shift+/" on a US ANSI
350. // keyboard.
351. func (k Key) String() string {
352. 	return uv.Key(k).String()
353. }
354. 
355. // Keystroke returns the keystroke representation of the [Key]. While less type
356. // safe than looking at the individual fields, it will usually be more
357. // convenient and readable to use this method when matching against keys.
358. //
359. // Note that modifier keys are always printed in the following order:
360. //   - ctrl

arguments.command:
cd /home/jcarlsson/.wasa/worktrees/0c1bb6a9d11f/task-141-upgrade-the-tui-to-new-bubble-tea-v2 && python <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
root = Path('.')
replacements = {
    'github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea': 'charm.land/bubbletea/v2',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput': 'charm.land/bubbles/v2/textinput',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/viewport': 'charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/key': 'charm.land/bubbles/v2/key',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss': 'charm.land/lipgloss/v2',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/table': 'charm.land/lipgloss/v2/table',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/tree': 'charm.land/lipgloss/v2/tree',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/list': 'charm.land/lipgloss/v2/list',
}
for path in root.joinpath('internal/tui').rglob('*.go'):
    text = path.read_text()
    orig = text
    for a,b in replacements.items():
        text = text.replace(a,b)
    text = text.replace('tea.KeyMsg', 'tea.KeyPressMsg')
    text = text.replace('tea.WithAltScreen()', '')
    text = text.replace('in.Width =', 'in.SetWidth(')
    text = text.replace('q.Width =', 'q.SetWidth(')
    text = text.replace('name.Width =', 'name.SetWidth(')
    text = text.replace('viewport.New(0, 0)', 'viewport.New(viewport.WithWidth(0), viewport.WithHeight(0))')
    text = text.replace('case " ":', 'case "space":')
    if text != orig:
        # fix parens from SetWidth replacements
        text = text.replace('in.SetWidth( max', 'in.SetWidth(max')
        text = text.replace('q.SetWidth( width', 'q.SetWidth(width')
        text = text.replace('name.SetWidth( width', 'name.SetWidth(width')
        path.write_text(text)
PY
arguments.description: Bulk replace Charm imports and basic v2 APIs

    'github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea': 'charm.land/bubbletea/v2',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput': 'charm.land/bubbles/v2/textinput',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/viewport': 'charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/key': 'charm.land/bubbles/v2/key',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss': 'charm.land/lipgloss/v2',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/table': 'charm.land/lipgloss/v2/table',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/tree': 'charm.land/lipgloss/v2/tree',
    'github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/list': 'charm.land/lipgloss/v2/list',
}
for path in root.joinpath('internal/tui').rglob('*.go'):
    text = path.read_text()
    orig = text
    for a,b in replacements.items():
        text = text.replace(a,b)
    text = text.replace('tea.KeyMsg', 'tea.KeyPressMsg')
    text = text.replace('tea.WithAltScreen(),', '')
    text = text.replace('tea.WithAltScreen()', '')
    text = text.replace('in.Width = max', 'in.SetWidth(max')
    text = text.replace('q.Width = width', 'q.SetWidth(width')
    text = text.replace('name.Width = width', 'name.SetWidth(width')
    text = text.replace('viewport.New(0, 0)', 'viewport.New(viewport.WithWidth(0), viewport.WithHeight(0))')
    text = text.replace('case " ":', 'case "space":')
    if text != orig:
        path.write_text(text)
PY","description":"Bulk replace Charm imports and basic v2 APIs","shellId":"bulk-replace2","initial_wait":30}

165. // Run launches the cockpit over reg and blocks until the user quits. It uses the
166. // alternate screen so the terminal is restored on exit and around every attach.
167. // cfg is the resolved cockpit configuration.
168. func Run(
169. 	home string,
170. 	reg *registry.Registry,
171. 	currentID string,
172. 	cfg config.Config,
173. ) error {
174. 	_, err := tea.NewProgram(
175. 		New(home, reg, currentID, cfg), 
176. 	).Run()
177. 	return err
178. }
